features.name: /clear
features.summary_ja: 会話履歴をクリアして新しい状態から始める
features.description_ja: 現在のセッションの会話履歴をクリア。メモリ（永続化された情報）は保持される。長時間のセッションで文脈をリセットしたい時に使う。
features.summary_en: Clear the current session's conversation history and start fresh; saved memory and persistent information are retained across sessions; Clears the conversation history of the current session while preserving any saved.
features.description_en: Clears the conversation history of the current session while preserving any saved memory and persistent information. Useful when you want to reset context during long-running sessions without losing important stored data. The cleared history cannot be recovered, so use this command when you are ready to start a new conversation thread within the same session. Note that clearing history is irreversible within the same session, so confirm you no longer need the prior context before running this command. It is particularly helpful before switching to a completely different task to avoid confusion from stale references in the context window.

features.name: /help
features.summary_ja: 利用可能なコマンド一覧を表示
features.description_ja: Claude Code で使える組み込みコマンドの一覧を表示。困ったらまずこれ。
features.summary_en: Display a list of all built-in slash commands and keyboard shortcuts available in the current Claude Code session; Shows a list of built-in commands available in Claude Code.
features.description_en: Shows a list of built-in commands available in Claude Code. When in doubt, start here. The help output covers slash commands, keyboard shortcuts, and basic usage tips, making it the fastest way to discover what you can do in the current session. This is the recommended first step when you are unsure what commands exist or have forgotten a specific command name. The output is especially useful on first setup or after an update that may have introduced new commands.

features.name: /memory
features.summary_ja: メモリーシステムを管理
features.description_ja: ユーザーの好み、プロジェクトの背景、フィードバックなどを永続的に蓄積するメモリーシステムを管理。将来の対話で自動的に参照される。
features.summary_en: Manage the persistent memory system that accumulates user preferences, project background, and feedback for use in future sessions; Manages the memory system that persistently accumulates user preferences, project background, feedback.
features.description_en: Manages the memory system that persistently accumulates user preferences, project background, feedback, and other information. Stored memories are automatically referenced in future interactions, allowing Claude to maintain context across sessions. Use this command to view, edit, or clear stored memories. Memory files are stored under ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/memory/ and indexed by MEMORY.md. You can open the memory viewer directly with /memory to read, edit, or delete specific stored facts without affecting the main conversation. You can also edit memory files directly at ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/memory/. Claude automatically references the most relevant memory entries when starting tasks, making subsequent sessions faster to context-load.

features.name: /agents
features.summary_ja: サブエージェントを管理
features.description_ja: サブエージェントの定義・起動・状態確認。複雑なタスクを並列実行する際に使う。Task tool と組み合わせる。
features.summary_en: Define, launch, and check the status of subagents for running complex tasks in parallel using the Task tool; Defines, launches, and checks the status of subagents.
features.description_en: Defines, launches, and checks the status of subagents. Used when running complex tasks in parallel. Works in combination with the Task tool. Subagents operate in separate contexts, enabling specialized parallel workloads without consuming the main session's context window. Use /agents to view all defined agents, check their running status, and confirm which agents are currently active. Combining this command with the Task tool gives you full visibility into your parallel workload. The /agents dialog also shows which agent types are defined in AGENTS.md. Review agent definitions before launching to ensure they match the task requirements.

features.name: /context
features.summary_ja: コンテキストウィンドウ使用率を表示
features.description_ja: 現在のセッションでのコンテキスト使用率を確認。長時間セッションでの圧縮タイミング判断に役立つ。
features.summary_en: Display the current context window usage percentage to help decide when to compact during long-running sessions; Shows the context usage rate for the current session.
features.description_en: Shows the context usage rate for the current session. Helps you decide when to use /compact during long sessions. Monitoring context usage prevents unexpected compaction and lets you plan ahead for large tasks that may require resetting or summarizing the conversation. If context usage is approaching the limit, consider running /compact to summarize and free up space before Claude automatically triggers compaction, which may discard context you still need. Proactively compressing context with /compact before it auto-triggers gives you more control over what is preserved. Pair this with the PreCompact hook to archive raw history first.

features.name: /hooks
features.summary_ja: フック設定を管理
features.description_ja: PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop等のイベントで自動実行するシェルスクリプトを設定。
features.summary_en: Configure shell scripts that run automatically on events such as PreToolUse, PostToolUse, and Stop for automation; Configures shell scripts that run automatically on events such as PreToolUse, PostToolUse, and Stop.
features.description_en: Configures shell scripts that run automatically on events such as PreToolUse, PostToolUse, and Stop. Hooks let you enforce policies, log activity, auto-format code, or send notifications without manual intervention. Use matchers to target specific tools. Hooks are configured in settings.json under the hooks key and are version-controlled when placed in .claude/settings.json. Use /hooks to review active hooks without editing the file manually. Hooks defined in .claude/settings.json are project-scoped and shared with the team. Personal hooks go in ~/.claude/settings.json. Both sets run together when a matching event fires.

features.name: /init
features.summary_ja: CLAUDE.md を初期化
features.description_ja: 現在のリポジトリを分析して CLAUDE.md（プロジェクトガイダンスファイル）の雛形を生成。
features.summary_en: Analyze the current repository and generate a CLAUDE.md project guidance file template for coding conventions and instructions; Analyzes the current repository and generates a CLAUDE.md template (project guidance file).
features.description_en: Analyzes the current repository and generates a CLAUDE.md template (project guidance file). CLAUDE.md is automatically loaded by Claude on every session start, making it the best place to document coding conventions, directory structure, and project-specific instructions. After generation, review the CLAUDE.md file and customize it with project-specific rules before committing it to your repository. Re-run /init to regenerate if the codebase changes significantly. Keep CLAUDE.md concise and focused on information unique to your project. Overly long CLAUDE.md files consume significant context budget on every session start.

features.name: /review
features.summary_ja: プルリクエストをレビュー
features.description_ja: 現在のブランチまたは指定PRをレビュー。コード品質、バグ可能性、改善提案を返す。
features.summary_en: Review the current branch or a specified pull request and return code quality findings, bug risks, and improvement suggestions; Reviews the current branch or a specified PR.
features.description_en: Reviews the current branch or a specified PR. Returns code quality assessments, potential bug findings, and improvement suggestions. Ideal for pre-merge checks or getting a second opinion on your changes before opening a pull request. The review output includes specific file and line references wherever possible. For security-focused checks, use /security-review instead, which applies a different lens focused on vulnerabilities. Review comments reference specific file paths and line numbers. Save the output with /copy or /export to create a reviewable artifact for your team.

features.name: /config
features.summary_ja: 設定を表示・変更
features.description_ja: テーマ、モデル、その他のユーザー設定を変更。settings.json の代替インタフェース。
features.summary_en: View and interactively change user settings such as theme and model; serves as an alternative to editing settings.json directly; Changes theme, model, and other user settings.
features.description_en: Changes theme, model, and other user settings. Serves as an interactive alternative to editing settings.json directly. Changes take effect immediately in the current session, and persistent changes are written back to your settings file automatically. Settings changed via /config are written to ~/.claude/settings.json for global changes or .claude/settings.local.json for project-level overrides, depending on your current scope. The interactive picker is easier to use than editing JSON directly, especially for discovering available options. Use it to explore what settings are configurable before writing settings.json manually.

features.name: /model
features.summary_ja: 使用するモデルを切り替え
features.description_ja: Opus / Sonnet / Haiku など、現在のセッションで使用するモデルを切り替える。
features.summary_en: Switch the model used in the current session between Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, and other available Claude model variants; Switches the model used in the current session among Opus, Sonnet, Haiku.
features.description_en: Switches the model used in the current session among Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, and other available options. Model changes take effect immediately for the next message. You can also pin a default model in settings.json or via the --model CLI flag. Note that some models may not be available depending on your authentication method or subscription plan. The /fast command provides a separate toggle for the high-speed Opus mode. The chosen model persists for the rest of the current session. Claude Code remembers your last-used model and offers it as the default on next launch.

features.name: /status
features.summary_ja: セッション状態を表示
features.description_ja: 現在のセッションの統計情報（モデル、ディレクトリ、git状態など）を表示。
features.summary_en: Display current session statistics including the active model, working directory, git state, and other session metadata; Shows statistical information for the current session, including the active model, working directory, and.
features.description_en: Shows statistical information for the current session, including the active model, working directory, and git status. Useful for a quick sanity check when you are unsure of the session state or want to confirm configuration before starting a long task. Running /status at the start of a session is a good habit when debugging unexpected behavior, as it confirms which settings and directories are actually active. Status also shows whether any extra directories have been added with /add-dir and the active permission mode. Use it after changing settings to confirm they took effect.

features.name: /cost
features.summary_ja: API利用コストを表示
features.description_ja: 現在のセッションでのAPIトークン使用量とコストを確認。
features.summary_en: Display the API token usage and estimated cost accumulated in the current session for monitoring spend; Shows the API token usage and estimated cost for the current session.
features.description_en: Shows the API token usage and estimated cost for the current session. Useful for monitoring spend during long working sessions or when evaluating the cost impact of different workflows. See /usage for a combined view including historical data. Token counts cover input, output, and cache tokens. For historical aggregated usage, use /usage or /stats, which provide broader views beyond the current session. Cost display uses approximate pricing based on published token rates. Actual charges may differ slightly due to caching discounts and plan-specific pricing adjustments.

features.name: /compact
features.summary_ja: コンテキストを強制圧縮
features.description_ja: コンテキストが膨らんだとき、過去の会話を要約して圧縮。重要な情報は保持。
features.summary_en: Force-compress the context window by summarizing past conversation when it grows large, preserving important information throughout; When the context window grows large, summarizes past conversation to compress it.
features.description_en: When the context window grows large, summarizes past conversation to compress it. Important information is preserved in the summary. Use /context to check usage before compacting, and configure the PreCompact hook to save data before compression runs. Compaction uses Claude's own summarization to condense history, so some detail may be lost. The PreCompact hook lets you save a raw snapshot before this happens. Compaction happens automatically when context usage reaches a threshold. You can adjust this threshold in settings.json or trigger it manually here before the threshold is reached.

features.name: /resume
features.summary_ja: 前回のセッションを再開
features.description_ja: 終了したセッションを再開して続きから作業を再開。
features.summary_en: Resume a terminated session and pick up from where you left off by selecting from a list of recent sessions.
features.description_en: Resumes a terminated session and picks up where you left off. Presents a list of recent sessions to choose from. You can also resume a specific session from the CLI with claude --resume <session-id> or use /resume inside an active session. Sessions are stored locally and remain available until explicitly deleted. The list view shows session names if you have used /rename, making it easier to identify the right session. You can rename sessions with /rename before exiting so they are easier to identify in the resume list. Sessions are retained indefinitely unless you delete them.

features.name: /security-review
features.summary_ja: セキュリティレビューを実施
features.description_ja: 現在のブランチの変更内容に対してセキュリティ観点のレビューを実行。
features.summary_en: Perform a security-focused review of the changes in the current branch, identifying vulnerabilities and insecure patterns; Runs a security-oriented review of the changes in the current branch.
features.description_en: Runs a security-oriented review of the changes in the current branch. Identifies potential vulnerabilities, insecure patterns, and risky dependencies. Complements /review by focusing specifically on security aspects rather than general code quality. The review covers common vulnerability categories including injection flaws, insecure dependencies, secrets in code, and authentication issues. Results are returned as an annotated diff view. Security review output includes severity ratings so you can prioritize the most critical findings. Share results with your security team by exporting via /export.

features.name: /plugin
features.summary_ja: プラグインを管理
features.description_ja: プラグインのインストール・アンインストール・更新・一覧表示・エラー確認を行う。Marketplace からのインストールや --plugin-dir で読み込んだプラグインの管理に使う。
features.summary_en: Install, uninstall, update, list, and check errors for plugins from the Marketplace or loaded via --plugin-dir; Installs, uninstalls, updates, lists, and checks errors for plugins.
features.description_en: Installs, uninstalls, updates, lists, and checks errors for plugins. Used for managing plugins installed from the Marketplace or loaded via --plugin-dir. After updating a plugin, run /reload-plugins to apply changes immediately without restarting the session. Plugins installed from the Marketplace are managed separately from local plugins loaded with --plugin-dir. Use /reload-plugins after any local plugin change to pick up edits without restarting. After installing a new plugin, Claude Code detects and loads it automatically for the next session. Use /reload-plugins in the current session if you need it immediately without restarting.

features.name: /skills
features.summary_ja: 利用可能なスキルを一覧・検索
features.description_ja: インストール済みのスキルを一覧表示。テキスト入力でフィルタリング可能。スキルを選択するとプロンプトに `/skill-name` が補完される。
features.summary_en: List and search installed skills with a filterable picker; selecting a skill autocompletes its slash command into the prompt; Lists installed skills with a filterable text input.
features.description_en: Lists installed skills with a filterable text input. Selecting a skill autocompletes /skill-name into the prompt. Skills are reusable prompt-based capabilities defined in SKILL.md files and can be triggered manually here or automatically when their description keywords match your input. Skills differ from custom slash commands in that they are full SKILL.md-based modules with triggers, tools, and logic. Use /skills to explore what is currently installed before invoking one manually. Skill descriptions appear in the picker to help you identify the right skill for your task. Selecting a skill from /skills does not immediately execute it; it just fills the prompt.

features.name: /mcp
features.summary_ja: MCPサーバーの接続状態を確認
features.description_ja: 接続中の MCP サーバー一覧・ツール数・認証状態を表示。0ツールのサーバーはフラグ表示される。重複URLサーバーの警告も表示。
features.summary_en: Check the connection status, tool counts, and authentication state of all connected MCP servers in the current session; Displays the list of connected MCP servers, their tool counts, and authentication.
features.description_en: Displays the list of connected MCP servers, their tool counts, and authentication status. Servers with zero tools are flagged. Warnings about duplicate URL servers are also displayed. Use this as the first stop when troubleshooting MCP connectivity issues. Connection issues often show up here as servers with zero tools or authentication errors. Cross-reference with /doctor for a broader health check that includes plugin and version warnings. Servers showing zero tools may have failed to connect or may have been misconfigured. Check the server URL, authentication, and network access if a server is not showing expected tools.

features.name: /doctor
features.summary_ja: 環境の診断・問題チェック
features.description_ja: MCP サーバーのエラー、無効化されたプラグインの警告、バージョン競合などの問題を診断して表示。トラブルシュートの第一歩。
features.summary_en: Diagnose environment problems including MCP server errors, disabled plugin warnings, and version conflicts as a first troubleshooting step; Diagnoses and displays problems such as MCP server errors, disabled plugin warnings.
features.description_en: Diagnoses and displays problems such as MCP server errors, disabled plugin warnings, and version conflicts. The first step in troubleshooting issues. Run /doctor whenever something is not working as expected before diving into deeper investigation. Run /doctor after installing a new plugin, upgrading Claude Code, or changing MCP configuration. It surfaces problems that would otherwise require manual log inspection to find. The /doctor output is the fastest way to get a summary of all active warnings in one place. Share the output when filing a support request to give context about your environment.

features.name: /usage
features.summary_ja: トークン使用量とコストを確認
features.description_ja: 現在セッションおよび過去の API トークン使用量・コストを表示。/cost と /stats のタブも /usage 内から開ける（v2.1.118 で統合）。
features.summary_en: View current and historical API token usage and costs in one unified place, with access to cost and stats tabs.
features.description_en: Displays current session and historical API token usage and costs. The /cost and /stats tabs are also accessible from within /usage (unified in v2.1.118). Use the tabs to switch between real-time cost tracking and longer-term usage statistics. The unified /usage view replaces separate /cost and /stats commands as a single dashboard. Historical data is grouped by day and model so you can track spending trends over time. You can switch between the Cost and Stats tabs inside /usage to get both real-time and historical views without leaving the dialog. Press Esc to close the dialog.

features.name: /effort
features.summary_ja: Thinking の処理強度を調整
features.description_ja: Claude の思考量（effort）を low / normal / high / max / auto から選択。Opus モデルの xhigh も設定可能。速度と精度のトレードオフを制御。
features.summary_en: Adjust Claude's thinking effort level from low to max to control the speed-versus-accuracy tradeoff for the session; Selects Claude's thinking effort (effort level) from low, normal, high, max, or auto.
features.description_en: Selects Claude's thinking effort (effort level) from low, normal, high, max, or auto. The xhigh setting is also available for Opus models. Controls the speed-versus-accuracy tradeoff: lower effort is faster and cheaper, while higher effort produces more thorough responses. The auto level lets Claude decide effort based on query complexity, which is a good default. Use max for deep architectural questions and low for simple factual queries to optimize cost. Effort settings are session-scoped and reset to auto on next launch unless configured in settings.json. Use max effort sparingly since it significantly increases both latency and token cost.

features.name: /rename
features.summary_ja: セッションに名前をつける
features.description_ja: 現在のセッションにわかりやすい名前を付ける。ステータスバーに表示され、/resume での再開時に識別しやすくなる。
features.summary_en: Assign a descriptive human-readable name to the current session so it is easy to identify when resuming later; Assigns a human-readable name to the current session.
features.description_en: Assigns a human-readable name to the current session. The name appears in the status bar and makes it easier to identify the session when resuming with /resume. Helpful when managing multiple parallel sessions or returning to old sessions after a break. Session names are visible in the /resume picker and the status bar, making them essential when running multiple parallel sessions. Names persist after the session ends for future resumption. Renaming a session is especially useful in team environments where multiple engineers share the same machine. Names appear in session logs alongside timestamps for easy audit.

features.name: /login
features.summary_ja: OAuth 認証を再実行
features.description_ja: トークン期限切れ・認証エラー時に OAuth ログインを再実行。CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN 環境変数が設定されていてもディスク認証に切り替えられる。
features.summary_en: Re-run OAuth authentication to fix expired tokens or authentication errors, switching from environment-variable to disk-based auth if needed; Re-runs OAuth login when tokens expire or authentication errors occur.
features.description_en: Re-runs OAuth login when tokens expire or authentication errors occur. Even if the CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN environment variable is set, this command can switch to disk-based authentication. Use it whenever you see authentication errors in an active session. After re-login, the new token is stored on disk and takes precedence over any environment variable. If login fails in a restricted environment, check that the required OAuth endpoints are accessible. If you are using API key authentication rather than OAuth, /login is not relevant. It applies only to accounts that use the browser-based OAuth flow for authentication.

features.name: /permissions
features.summary_ja: 許可・拒否されたコマンドを管理
features.description_ja: Auto モードで拒否されたコマンドの履歴を確認・リトライ。許可ルールの追加・削除も可能。Recent タブで直近の許可状況を確認。
features.summary_en: View the history of commands blocked in Auto mode, retry them, and add or remove permanent allow rules; Shows the history of commands blocked in Auto mode and lets you.
features.description_en: Shows the history of commands blocked in Auto mode and lets you retry them or add permanent allow rules. The Recent tab shows recent permission decisions. Use this to fine-tune which commands Claude can run automatically without prompting. Rules added here are written to .claude/settings.json or settings.local.json. The Recent tab is useful for auditing what Claude has been allowed to run automatically during the session. Reviewing permissions periodically is a good security practice. Remove overly broad allow rules that were added temporarily and are no longer needed.

features.name: /add-dir
features.summary_ja: 作業ディレクトリを追加
features.description_ja: 現在のセッションに追加のディレクトリを読み込む。--remember オプションで永続化可能。複数リポジトリにまたがる作業に便利。
features.summary_en: Load an additional working directory into the current session, with an option to persist the addition permanently; Loads an additional directory into the current session.
features.description_en: Loads an additional directory into the current session. The --remember option persists the addition across future sessions. Useful when working across multiple repositories or monorepo packages that live outside the current working directory. Directories added this way are accessible to all tools including Bash and Read within the same session. Use --remember to avoid repeating the command on every session start for frequently used paths. Added directories are listed in /status so you can always check which extra directories are in scope. Remove a directory by restarting the session without the --add-dir flag.

features.name: /branch
features.summary_ja: セッションをブランチ（分岐）する
features.description_ja: 現在のセッションをフォークして別の方向を試す。分岐したセッションは /resume で再開可能。実験的な変更を本流に影響させずに試すのに使う。
features.summary_en: Fork the current session to try a different approach without affecting the main thread; resume branches with /resume; Forks the current session to try a different direction.
features.description_en: Forks the current session to try a different direction. The branched session can be resumed later with /resume. Use this to experiment with risky or alternative approaches without affecting the main session. Branching creates an independent copy of the conversation state. Branching is non-destructive; the original session remains intact. You can branch multiple times from the same point and compare approaches before deciding which direction to pursue. Each branch is a full copy of the session state at the point of branching. Branches do not share file changes, so edits in one branch do not affect others.

features.name: /copy
features.summary_ja: 最後の応答をクリップボードにコピー
features.description_ja: Claude の直前の応答をクリップボードにコピー。マークダウン形式で GitHub・Notion・Slack に貼り付けるのに最適。tmux 環境でも動作（/terminal-setup 要設定）。
features.summary_en: Copy Claude's most recent response to the clipboard in markdown format for pasting into GitHub, Notion, or Slack; Copies Claude's most recent response to the clipboard in markdown format.
features.description_en: Copies Claude's most recent response to the clipboard in markdown format. Ideal for pasting into GitHub, Notion, or Slack. Also works in tmux environments when /terminal-setup has been configured. Use /terminal-setup first if clipboard access is not working. The clipboard output preserves markdown formatting, including code blocks and headings. If copying fails in a headless terminal, run /terminal-setup first to configure the required clipboard access. If the last response includes code blocks, they are preserved with triple-backtick fencing in the copied output. This makes it easy to paste directly into documentation or issue trackers.

features.name: /remote-control
features.summary_ja: ブラウザ・スマホからセッションを継続
features.description_ja: CLI セッションを claude.ai/code に接続し、ブラウザやスマートフォンから作業を引き継げる。Remote Control 接続中はセッションカラーも同期される。
features.summary_en: Connect the CLI session to claude.ai/code so work can continue from a browser or smartphone seamlessly; Connects the CLI session to claude.ai/code, allowing you to hand off work to a.
features.description_en: Connects the CLI session to claude.ai/code, allowing you to hand off work to a browser or smartphone. Session color is synchronized during Remote Control connections. Useful for reviewing or continuing work when you are away from your terminal. Remote Control is useful when you start a task on one device and want to continue reviewing or guiding it from another. The connection is secure and tied to your claude.ai account. The Remote Control QR code is displayed in the terminal when you run the command. Scan it with the claude.ai mobile app to connect instantly without typing a URL.

features.name: /terminal-setup
features.summary_ja: ターミナル環境を最適化
features.description_ja: Claude Code が最適動作するようターミナルを設定。iTerm2 のクリップボードアクセス許可・スクロール感度調整などを自動設定する。
features.summary_en: Configure the terminal environment for optimal Claude Code operation, including iTerm2 clipboard access and scroll sensitivity settings; Configures the terminal for optimal Claude Code operation.
features.description_en: Configures the terminal for optimal Claude Code operation. Automatically sets up iTerm2 clipboard access permissions, scroll sensitivity adjustments, and other settings. Run this once when setting up a new terminal environment or if clipboard features are not working. Run this command once during initial setup rather than each session. Settings are written to your terminal configuration file so they persist across restarts. If clipboard operations still fail after running /terminal-setup, check that your terminal emulator has the required permissions to access the system clipboard.

features.name: /reload-plugins
features.summary_ja: プラグインをリロード
features.description_ja: インストール済みプラグインを再読み込み。プラグインを編集・更新した後に変更を即反映させる。依存パッケージの自動インストールも行う。
features.summary_en: Reload all installed plugins to apply edits or updates immediately without restarting the session; auto-installs dependencies; Reloads installed plugins to reflect changes immediately after editing or updating them.
features.description_en: Reloads installed plugins to reflect changes immediately after editing or updating them. Also automatically installs any required dependency packages. Use after modifying a local plugin during development to pick up changes without restarting the session. This is faster than restarting Claude Code when iterating on a plugin under development. Dependency auto-install means you do not need to manually run npm install after adding a new package. Dependency installation during /reload-plugins follows the same process as the initial install. If a dependency fails to install, the plugin is disabled and an error is shown in /doctor.

features.name: /color
features.summary_ja: セッションのアクセントカラーを設定
features.description_ja: 現在のセッションのUI色を変更。引数なしで実行するとランダムに選択。Remote Control 接続時は claude.ai/code にも同期される。
features.summary_en: Set the UI accent color for the current session randomly or by name; syncs to claude.ai during Remote Control; Changes the UI accent color for the current session.
features.description_en: Changes the UI accent color for the current session. Running without arguments selects a random color. When connected via Remote Control, the color is also synchronized to claude.ai/code. Use /color <name> to specify a particular color. Colors are session-scoped by default and reset on next launch unless persisted via settings. The Remote Control sync makes it easy to identify which session you are viewing from a mobile device. Use a distinct color for each active session when running multiple Claude Code instances. This visual differentiation makes it easy to identify sessions in split-terminal layouts.

features.name: /loop
features.summary_ja: プロンプトを定期実行スケジュール
features.description_ja: 指定した間隔でプロンプトやスラッシュコマンドを繰り返し実行。/loop 5m デプロイ確認 のように使う。Esc でキャンセル可能。`/proactive` はエイリアス（v2.1.105）。Bedrock/Vertex でも利用可。

examples.title: Check deploy every 5 minutes
examples.code: /loop 5m デプロイ状態を確認して

features.summary_en: Schedule a prompt or slash command to repeat automatically on a specified interval; press Esc to cancel the loop; Repeatedly executes a prompt or slash command at a specified interval.
features.description_en: Repeatedly executes a prompt or slash command at a specified interval. For example: /loop 5m check deploy status. Press Esc to cancel. /proactive is an alias (v2.1.105). Also works on Bedrock and Vertex. Useful for polling-style automation without writing scripts. Unlike cron-style scheduling, /loop runs within your current interactive session and stops when you press Esc. For persistent scheduled jobs that run without your session, see /schedule instead. Interval formats accepted include 5s, 10m, 2h, and combinations like 1m30s. Claude does not count tool execution time against the interval, so loops run at consistent wall-clock intervals.

features.name: /theme
features.summary_ja: UIテーマを切り替え・カスタマイズ
features.description_ja: テーマピッカーを開きビルトインテーマを選択、または ~/.claude/themes/ のJSONファイルで独自テーマを作成。Auto(match terminal)でダーク/ライト自動切替も可。Ctrl+t でシンタックスハイライトをON/OFF。
features.summary_en: Open the theme picker to select a built-in theme or create a custom one via JSON files in ~/.claude/themes/; Opens the theme picker to select a built-in theme, or create.
features.description_en: Opens the theme picker to select a built-in theme, or create a custom theme via a JSON file in ~/.claude/themes/. Auto (match terminal) mode switches automatically between dark and light. Toggle syntax highlighting with Ctrl+t. Custom JSON themes allow fine-grained control over syntax highlighting colors, UI element colors, and font styles. Themes are loaded from ~/.claude/themes/ and available immediately after saving. Themes affect syntax highlighting in code blocks, UI chrome colors, and prompt area styling. Switching themes takes effect immediately without restarting the session.

features.name: /stats
features.summary_ja: APIトークン使用統計を表示
features.description_ja: お気に入りモデル・使用量グラフ・使用ストリーク等の詳細統計を表示。v2.1.118 以降は /usage の Stats タブへのショートカットとして動作。r キーで Last 7 days / Last 30 days / All time を切替。
features.summary_en: Display detailed API token usage statistics, favorite models, usage graphs, and streak data; shortcut to the Stats tab in /usage.
features.description_en: Shows detailed statistics including favorite models, usage graphs, and usage streaks. Since v2.1.118 it acts as a shortcut to the Stats tab within /usage. Press r to cycle between Last 7 days, Last 30 days, and All time views. Streak data tracks consecutive days of Claude Code usage and is displayed alongside model usage breakdowns. The r key cycles the time range without leaving the stats view. Stats data is collected from session logs stored locally. If you reset or delete session logs, historical stats are lost. Export stats data before clearing logs if you need them.

features.name: /feedback
features.summary_ja: フィードバックをAnthropicに送信
features.description_ja: バグ報告・機能要望をAnthropicに直接送信し、GitHub issue URL も生成。認証状態によって利用不可の場合はメニューにその旨が表示される。長い説明文も正しく処理される（v2.1.14 修正済）。
features.summary_en: Send bug reports or feature requests directly to Anthropic and generate a GitHub issue URL for tracking; Sends bug reports and feature requests directly to Anthropic and generates a GitHub.
features.description_en: Sends bug reports and feature requests directly to Anthropic and generates a GitHub issue URL. If unavailable due to authentication state, the menu indicates why. Long descriptions are handled correctly (fixed in v2.1.14). The fastest way to report issues you encounter. Feedback submitted via this command is directly reviewed by the Claude Code team. For urgent bugs, include a session export from /export to provide full context. Feedback submitted with /feedback is anonymous by default unless you include identifying information in the message. Bug reports with session exports are processed faster than text-only reports.

features.name: /voice
features.summary_ja: 音声入力（ボイスモード）を切替
features.description_ja: 音声でプロンプトを入力するボイスモードをON/OFF。有効化時に認識言語を表示し、未対応言語は警告。Remote Control セッションでも動作。認証プランによっては非表示になる。
features.summary_en: Toggle voice input mode on or off to enter prompts by speaking; displays the recognized language when enabled; Toggles voice input mode on and off.
features.description_en: Toggles voice input mode on and off. When enabled, the recognized language is displayed and unsupported languages show a warning. Also works in Remote Control sessions. May be hidden depending on your authentication plan. Voice input transcription runs locally and requires microphone access. For languages showing a warning, switch to text input or check if the language is supported in your operating environment. Voice input converts speech to text before sending the prompt. The transcription is displayed in the input field so you can review and edit it before submitting.

features.name: /tui
features.summary_ja: フリッカーフリーなフルスクリーンレンダラーに切替
features.description_ja: /tui fullscreen で代替レンダラーに切り替える。フリッカーなし・低メモリ使用量・マウスサポート・選択時の自動コピーを備える。worktree内セッションでも利用可能。
features.summary_en: Switch to the flicker-free fullscreen renderer with low memory usage, mouse support, and automatic copy-on-selection; Switches to an alternative renderer with /tui fullscreen.
features.description_en: Switches to an alternative renderer with /tui fullscreen. Features flicker-free rendering, low memory usage, mouse support, and automatic copy on selection. Available in worktree sessions as well. Provides a more polished TUI experience for long coding sessions. The fullscreen renderer is particularly useful on slower machines or in environments with frequent terminal redraws. Auto-copy on selection eliminates the need to manually run /copy after each response. Switch back to the default renderer at any time by running /tui again to toggle off fullscreen mode. Both renderers support the same feature set; the difference is purely visual.

features.name: /extra-usage
features.summary_ja: 追加API利用を有効化するダイアログを開く
features.description_ja: プランの使用量上限を超えた追加API利用を有効化する。有効化後は /fast が即座に使用可能になる。VS Code および Remote Control（ブラウザ・スマホ）からも利用可能。
features.summary_en: Open a dialog to enable additional API usage beyond the plan limit; makes /fast available immediately after activation; Opens a dialog to enable additional API usage beyond your plan's limit.
features.description_en: Opens a dialog to enable additional API usage beyond your plan's limit. After activation, /fast becomes immediately available. Also accessible from VS Code and Remote Control (browser/smartphone). Renamed to /usage-credits in v2.1.144; the old name still works as an alias. Usage credits are billed at standard API rates above your plan allowance. After enabling, the credits balance is visible in /usage-credits and updates in real time as you use the API. Usage credits are consumed before your plan limit, not after. The balance resets each billing cycle. Check the /usage-credits dialog to see the current balance and consumption rate.

features.name: /rewind
features.summary_ja: 会話を巻き戻してコード変更を取り消す
features.description_ja: ピッカーで任意のポイントを選択して会話を巻き戻し、コード変更も元に戻せる。/undo はエイリアス。VS Code では Esc×2 でも起動可能。
features.summary_en: Select any prior conversation point using a picker to rewind the session and undo associated code changes; Uses a picker to select any earlier point in the conversation and rewinds.
features.description_en: Uses a picker to select any earlier point in the conversation and rewinds, also reverting code changes made after that point. /undo is an alias. In VS Code you can also trigger it with Esc×2. Use this to recover from a bad direction without starting a new session. Unlike /clear, rewind reverts file changes made during the session in addition to the conversation. This makes it a safe undo for multi-file edits that turned out to be the wrong approach.

features.name: /upgrade
features.summary_ja: Claude Maxプランへスムーズにアップグレード
features.description_ja: Claude Max プランへの切り替えダイアログを開く。現在の認証設定で利用できない場合は自動的に非表示になる。レート制限時にサジェストされる場合がある。
features.summary_en: Open the Claude Max plan upgrade dialog when available; may be suggested automatically during rate-limited sessions; Opens the Claude Max plan upgrade dialog.
features.description_en: Opens the Claude Max plan upgrade dialog. Automatically hidden when unavailable under the current authentication configuration. May be suggested when you hit rate limits. Use /extra-usage if you need additional usage without upgrading your plan. Upgrading to Claude Max unlocks higher rate limits and access to additional models. The dialog only appears when upgrade paths are available for your current account configuration. The upgrade flow opens claude.ai in a browser where you complete the subscription change. Return to the CLI session after the upgrade completes; no restart is required.

features.name: /exit
features.summary_ja: セッションを終了してアーカイブ
features.description_ja: 現在のセッションを終了しアーカイブする。Remote Control（ブラウザ・スマホ）からも実行可能。終了時にセッションが確実に保存される。
features.summary_en: End and archive the current session safely; also executable from Remote Control via browser or smartphone; Ends the current session and archives it.
features.description_en: Ends the current session and archives it. Can also be executed from Remote Control (browser/smartphone). Ensures the session is properly saved on exit. Use /resume to return to archived sessions later. Using /exit ensures the session is properly archived with metadata, making it findable via /resume. Closing the terminal without /exit may result in incomplete session records. Running /exit before closing the terminal is recommended over just closing the terminal. It ensures hooks like SessionEnd run properly and session metadata is written correctly.

features.name: /export
features.summary_ja: 会話をファイルにエクスポート
features.description_ja: 現在の会話を外部ファイルに書き出す。絶対パスや ~ も指定可能で、完了後にファイルの全パスを表示。会話の共有や記録保存に使う。
features.summary_en: Export the current conversation to a file at a specified path for sharing, archiving, or external reference; Writes the current conversation to an external file.
features.description_en: Writes the current conversation to an external file. Absolute paths and ~ are supported; the full path is displayed after completion. Useful for sharing conversations or keeping records. Works alongside /copy for getting conversation content out of Claude Code. Exports are plain text by default and include the full conversation including tool calls and file edits. Use /copy for quick one-response exports when you do not need the full history. The exported file captures the full conversation including tool calls, file edits, and error messages. Use it as an audit log for automated tasks or as context for a new session.

features.name: /ultrareview
features.summary_ja: クラウドで並列マルチエージェントコードレビュー
features.description_ja: クラウド上でマルチエージェントを並列実行し包括的なコードレビューを行う。引数なしで現在ブランチをレビュー、`/ultrareview <PR番号>` でGitHub PRを対象指定。`claude ultrareview` でCIからの非対話実行も可。
features.summary_en: Run a parallel multi-agent code review in the cloud for comprehensive coverage of the current branch or a specific PR.
features.description_en: Runs multiple agents in parallel on the cloud to perform a comprehensive code review. Without arguments, reviews the current branch; with /ultrareview <PR number> it targets a specific GitHub PR. Use claude ultrareview for non-interactive CI execution. The parallel multi-agent approach provides broader coverage than a single-agent review, catching issues that a single pass might miss. Results are returned as a consolidated report. Cloud compute scales with PR size, so large PRs with many changed files still complete in a reasonable time. Results are returned as a structured report with severity levels.

features.name: /plan
features.summary_ja: プランモードをプロンプトから直接起動
features.description_ja: プランモード（読み取り専用計画モード）をスラッシュコマンドで直接有効化する。説明文を引数に渡すと `/plan 認証バグを修正` のように即座に計画を開始できる。Shift+Tab でも同様に切り替え可能。
features.summary_en: Activate read-only plan mode directly from the prompt, optionally providing an inline description to start planning immediately; Activates plan mode (read-only planning mode) directly via a slash command.
features.description_en: Activates plan mode (read-only planning mode) directly via a slash command. Passing a description such as /plan fix auth bug immediately starts planning with that goal. Shift+Tab also toggles plan mode. Use this to have Claude map out an approach before writing any code. While in plan mode, Claude can read files and analyze code but will not write, edit, or execute anything. This makes it safe to explore an approach without side effects. Switch back to normal mode by pressing Shift+Tab or running /plan again. Claude indicates plan mode is active in the status bar so you always know which mode you are in.

features.name: /tasks
features.summary_ja: バックグラウンドタスクの状態を管理・確認
features.description_ja: バックグラウンドで実行中のタスクの状態を確認・管理するダイアログを開く。タスクが1つのみの場合は直接タスク詳細に遷移する。remote session URL なども確認できる。
features.summary_en: Open a management dialog to view and track the status of all running background tasks and remote sessions; Opens a dialog to view and manage the status of background tasks.
features.description_en: Opens a dialog to view and manage the status of background tasks. Navigates directly to task details when only one task is running. Remote session URLs and other task metadata are also visible here. The dialog shows task start time, status, token usage, and the remote session URL when applicable. Use this alongside /bg to monitor background agents you have launched. Task metadata includes start time, elapsed duration, and token counts per task. Click a task to expand its full log and see detailed tool call history for that background run.

features.name: /btw
features.summary_ja: 応答生成中にサイドクエスチョンを割り込ませる
features.description_ja: Claudeが応答を生成中に `/btw 質問` と入力してサイドの質問を差し込める。貼り付けたテキストも含めて処理され、メインの応答を中断せずに別の問いを解決できる。

examples.title: Side question
examples.code: /btw この関数の引数の型は？

features.summary_en: Inject a side question while Claude is generating a response, without interrupting or waiting for the main output; Allows you to insert a side question with /btw <question> while Claude.
features.description_en: Allows you to insert a side question with /btw <question> while Claude is generating a response. Pasted text is also included. Lets you resolve a secondary question without interrupting or waiting for the main response to finish. Side questions are processed in the same context as the ongoing response. This is useful for quick lookups like type signatures or API details while Claude is writing longer code. The /btw feature is useful during long code generation when you realize you need a quick clarification. Claude queues the side question and answers it once the main response finishes.

features.name: /insights
features.summary_ja: セッション履歴からインサイトレポートを生成
features.description_ja: 現在のセッション履歴を分析してインサイトレポートを生成し、レポートファイルへのリンクを返す。ツール呼び出し履歴も含めて処理される。セッションの振り返りや記録保存に使う。
features.summary_en: Analyze current session history and generate an insights report file with a link returned for easy access; Analyzes the current session history and generates an insights report, returning a link.
features.description_en: Analyzes the current session history and generates an insights report, returning a link to the report file. Tool call history is included in the analysis. Use for session retrospectives or to keep a structured record of what was accomplished. The insights report is saved to a file in the project directory so you can reference it outside Claude Code. Tool call summaries in the report make it useful for process retrospectives. The insights report includes a breakdown of tool calls, tokens used, and key decisions made during the session. Use it during retrospectives to identify inefficiencies in your workflow.

features.name: /release-notes
features.summary_ja: Claude Codeのリリースノートを確認
features.description_ja: Claude Code の過去バージョンのリリースノートをインタラクティブなバージョンピッカーで閲覧できる（v2.1.92以降）。最新版から任意バージョンを選択して更新内容を確認。
features.summary_en: Browse Claude Code release notes for any past version using an interactive version picker available since v2.1.92; Browse release notes for past versions of Claude Code using an interactive version.
features.description_en: Browse release notes for past versions of Claude Code using an interactive version picker (available since v2.1.92). Select any version from the latest downward to review what changed. Useful for understanding new features or diagnosing regressions after an update. This is the official changelog viewer built into Claude Code. You can also access the full changelog at https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog outside the app. Release notes are fetched from the embedded changelog rather than requiring an internet connection. They are always available offline, making this command reliable in air-gapped environments.

features.name: /keybindings
features.summary_ja: カスタムキーバインド設定を開始
features.description_ja: カスタムキーボードショートカットの設定ガイドを表示。コンテキストごとのキーバインド設定・コードシーケンス作成・~/.claude/keybindings.json の編集方法を案内する（v2.1.18で追加）。
features.summary_en: Start the custom keybinding setup guide covering per-context shortcuts, key sequences, and editing ~/.claude/keybindings.json; Displays a guide for configuring custom keyboard shortcuts.
features.description_en: Displays a guide for configuring custom keyboard shortcuts. Covers per-context keybinding settings, creating code sequences, and editing ~/.claude/keybindings.json (added in v2.1.18). For a quicker path, use the keybindings-help skill. The guide walks through creating context-aware bindings, meaning different keys can trigger different actions depending on whether you are in the editor or the prompt area. Keybindings are context-aware: you can bind the same key to different actions in the editor versus the prompt input area. The guide explains how to write context conditions in the JSON file.

features.name: /sandbox
features.summary_ja: サンドボックス実行環境を管理
features.description_ja: サンドボックス環境の状態を確認するタブ付きダイアログを表示。Dependencies タブで依存関係のインストール状況と手順を確認できる。Tab/矢印キーでタブ切替が可能。
features.summary_en: View the sandbox execution environment status in a tabbed dialog including dependency installation state and instructions; Shows a tabbed dialog for checking sandbox environment status.
features.description_en: Shows a tabbed dialog for checking sandbox environment status. The Dependencies tab shows installation status and instructions for required packages. Switch between tabs with Tab or arrow keys. Use this when sandbox-dependent features are not working as expected. The sandbox is used for running untrusted or experimental code in isolation. The Dependencies tab helps you install required packages into the sandbox environment before running code. The sandbox provides an isolated execution environment for testing code safely. Use the Dependencies tab before running any code to confirm required packages are installed and ready.

features.name: /claude-api
features.summary_ja: Claude API開発を支援するスキルを起動
features.description_ja: Claude API・Anthropic SDK を使ったアプリ開発を支援するスキル（v2.1.69で追加）。Managed Agents、エージェント設計パターン、ツール設計・コンテキスト管理・キャッシング戦略のガイダンスを提供。
features.summary_en: Launch a skill that assists with building applications using the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, and Managed Agents patterns; Launches a skill that helps with building applications using the Claude API.
features.description_en: Launches a skill that helps with building applications using the Claude API and Anthropic SDK (added in v2.1.69). Provides guidance on Managed Agents, agent design patterns, tool design, context management, and caching strategies. The skill is particularly helpful for questions about prompt caching, tool design, and migrating between Claude model versions. It covers both the Python and TypeScript SDKs. The skill provides ready-made code templates for common patterns including streaming responses, tool use, and multi-turn conversations. Templates target both Python and TypeScript.

features.name: /debug
features.summary_ja: デバッグログをセッション中に切り替え
features.description_ja: 現在のセッションのデバッグログをミッドセッションで切替（v2.1.30追加・v2.1.71更新）。デフォルトでは記録されないデバッグログをオンにしてトラブルシュートに使う。Claudeによるセッション診断にも対応。
features.summary_en: Toggle debug logging on or off mid-session to enable detailed output for troubleshooting unexpected behavior; Toggles debug logging for the current session mid-session (added in v2.1.30, updated in v2.1.71).
features.description_en: Toggles debug logging for the current session mid-session (added in v2.1.30, updated in v2.1.71). Debug logs are not recorded by default; enable them here for troubleshooting. Claude can also run session diagnostics when debug mode is active. Debug output includes detailed MCP connection logs, tool call traces, and internal event streams. Turn off debug logging when you are done to avoid performance overhead. Debug logs are written to the session log file at ~/.claude/logs/. You can tail this file in another terminal while Claude runs to watch events in real time.

features.name: /todos
features.summary_ja: 現在のTODOアイテム一覧を表示
features.description_ja: TodoWriteツールで記録された現在のセッションのTODOアイテムをオーバーレイで一覧表示（v1.0.94で追加）。pending/in_progress/completed の進捗状態を確認できる。
features.summary_en: Display an overlay listing all current TODO items tracked by the TodoWrite tool with pending, in-progress, and completed states; Displays current TODO items for the session as recorded by the.
features.description_en: Displays current TODO items for the session as recorded by the TodoWrite tool in an overlay (added in v1.0.94). Shows pending, in-progress, and completed states. Useful for quickly reviewing task progress without scrolling back through the conversation. The overlay updates in real time as Claude completes tasks. Items can be created by Claude via TodoWrite or added manually; the /todos overlay is the read-only view. TodoWrite creates items that persist across compaction events, so your task list survives even when conversation history is summarized. Completed items are archived but remain visible.

features.name: /recap
features.summary_ja: セッション再開時の作業コンテキストを要約
features.description_ja: セッションに戻った際に直前の作業内容のコンテキストを提供するリキャップ機能（v2.1.108追加）。/config で自動表示を設定でき、テレメトリ無効時は CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY 環境変数で強制有効化可能。
features.summary_en: Show a recap of recent work context when returning to a session; configurable for automatic display on session resume; Provides a recap of recent work context when returning to a.
features.description_en: Provides a recap of recent work context when returning to a session (added in v2.1.108). Auto-display can be configured via /config. When telemetry is disabled, force-enable with the CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY environment variable. The recap is generated from the session transcript and covers the most recent work before you went away. It is designed to be brief enough to scan quickly before resuming. The recap length is configurable: brief mode covers only the last few actions, while detailed mode summarizes the entire prior session. Configure your preference in /config.

features.name: /less-permission-prompts
features.summary_ja: 許可プロンプト削減の allowlist を自動提案
features.description_ja: 過去のトランスクリプトをスキャンして読み取り専用のBash・MCPツール呼び出しパターンを検出し、`.claude/settings.json` の許可リストとして優先順に提案するスキル（v2.1.111で追加）。
features.summary_en: Scan past transcripts to detect read-only tool patterns and propose a prioritized allowlist for settings.json; Scans past transcripts to detect read-only Bash and MCP tool call patterns, then proposes a.
features.description_en: Scans past transcripts to detect read-only Bash and MCP tool call patterns, then proposes a prioritized allowlist for .claude/settings.json (added in v2.1.111). Reduces repetitive permission prompts without broadly bypassing security. The generated allowlist entries use specific command matchers that are narrower than a broad bypass, preserving security while eliminating repetitive prompts for commands you always approve. Generated entries use specific Bash command matchers such as Bash(git status) rather than broad Bash(*), so you retain security for commands you have not explicitly approved.

features.name: /setup-bedrock
features.summary_ja: Amazon Bedrock接続を対話形式でセットアップ
features.description_ja: Amazon Bedrock 経由でClaudeを利用するための設定を対話形式で実施（v2.1.111改善）。CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR 設定時は実際のパスを表示し、既存ピンからモデル候補を提案、対応モデルでは1Mコンテキストオプションも提示。/setup-vertex も同様。
features.summary_en: Interactively configure Amazon Bedrock as the Claude provider with model suggestions and 1M context options; Interactively sets up Claude usage via Amazon Bedrock (improved in v2.1.111).
features.description_en: Interactively sets up Claude usage via Amazon Bedrock (improved in v2.1.111). Displays actual paths when CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is set, proposes model candidates from existing pins, and offers a 1M context option for supported models. /setup-vertex works similarly for Google Cloud. Bedrock requires valid AWS credentials in your environment before setup. The wizard detects your region and IAM profile automatically and validates connectivity before saving the configuration. After setup, Claude Code tests connectivity by sending a short probe request. If the probe fails, a detailed error message explains which IAM permissions or endpoint settings need adjustment.

features.name: /fast
features.summary_ja: Opus 4.6の高速応答モードを切り替え
features.description_ja: Claude Opus 4.6 ベースの高速応答モード（Fast Mode）をON/OFFする。サードパーティプロバイダーでは利用不可で「not available」と表示される。/extra-usage 有効化後は即座に使用可能になる（v2.1.37修正）。
features.summary_en: Toggle the Opus 4.6 fast response mode on or off; not available with third-party providers; Toggles Fast Mode (Claude Opus 4.6 based fast response) on and off.
features.description_en: Toggles Fast Mode (Claude Opus 4.6 based fast response) on and off. Not available with third-party providers; shows 'not available' in those cases. Becomes immediately usable after enabling /extra-usage (fixed in v2.1.37). Fast Mode reduces latency by using a smaller, optimized version of Opus 4.6. It is best for quick factual queries, short code edits, and interactive back-and-forth rather than deep analysis. Fast Mode availability depends on your subscription plan and the active model. The /extra-usage dialog shows whether Fast Mode is included in your current plan or requires credits.

features.name: /focus
features.summary_ja: フルスクリーンTUIのフォーカスビューを切り替え
features.description_ja: フルスクリーンレンダラー（/tui fullscreen）でのフォーカスビューをON/OFFする（v2.1.110追加）。Ctrl+O はノーマル/詳細ビューの切替専用に変更され、フォーカスビューはこのコマンドで制御する。フルスクリーン無効時は有効化方法を案内。
features.summary_en: Toggle the focus view in the fullscreen TUI renderer on or off; requires /tui fullscreen to be active first; Toggles the focus view on and off in the fullscreen renderer.
features.description_en: Toggles the focus view on and off in the fullscreen renderer (/tui fullscreen) (added in v2.1.110). Ctrl+O is now dedicated to switching between normal/detail views, and this command controls the focus view. If fullscreen is not enabled, instructions are shown on how to activate it. Focus view hides secondary UI panels to maximize the conversation area. It is designed for distraction-free reading or presenting Claude's output on a second screen. Focus view is particularly useful for reading long explanations or reviewing large diffs without being distracted by secondary panels. Toggle it on and off freely during a session.

features.name: /team-onboarding
features.summary_ja: チームメンバー向けオンボーディングガイドを生成
features.description_ja: ローカルの Claude Code 使用履歴をもとに、チームメンバーのランプアップガイドを自動生成する（v2.1.101追加）。プロジェクト固有のコマンドパターンや慣習をまとめたドキュメントを作成し、新規参加者の立ち上がりを支援する。
features.summary_en: Auto-generate a ramp-up guide for new team members based on local Claude Code usage history and project conventions; Automatically generates a ramp-up guide for new team members based on local.
features.description_en: Automatically generates a ramp-up guide for new team members based on local Claude Code usage history (added in v2.1.101). Creates a document summarizing project-specific command patterns and conventions to help new contributors get up to speed quickly. The generated guide is saved as a markdown file and can be committed to the repository. It captures implicit project knowledge that is often hard to document manually. The guide captures patterns that are project-specific and would otherwise only be learned through experience. Commit ONBOARDING.md to the repository so all new contributors benefit from it.

features.name: /install-github-app
features.summary_ja: GitHub App連携をインストール
features.description_ja: Claude Code と GitHub を連携する GitHub App のインストールを対話形式で実施。エラーハンドリングが強化されており（v1.0.7改善）、Esc でダイアログを閉じられる（v2.1.136修正）。GitHub Actions 連携のセットアップに使う。
features.summary_en: Interactively install the GitHub App integration for Claude Code to enable GitHub Actions and web connection features; Interactively installs the GitHub App that integrates Claude Code with GitHub.
features.description_en: Interactively installs the GitHub App that integrates Claude Code with GitHub. Error handling is improved (enhanced in v1.0.7); the dialog can be closed with Esc (fixed in v2.1.136). Used for setting up GitHub Actions integration. The GitHub App must be installed at the organization or repository level before Claude Code can interact with pull requests and issues via GitHub Actions. After installation, Claude Code can post review comments directly to pull requests and trigger CI workflows. The GitHub App requires read/write access to code and pull requests.

features.name: /simplify
features.summary_ja: 変更コードの品質・効率をレビューして修正
features.description_ja: v2.1.63で追加されたバンドル型スラッシュコマンド。変更されたコードを再利用性・品質・効率の観点でレビューし、検出した問題を修正する。コードレビューと実際のリファクタを一括で行う。
features.summary_en: Review changed code for reusability, quality, and efficiency then apply fixes in one bundled step; renamed to /code-review in v2.1.146.
features.description_en: A bundled slash command added in v2.1.63. Reviews changed code for reusability, quality, and efficiency, then applies fixes for detected issues. Combines code review and actual refactoring in a single command. Renamed to /code-review in v2.1.146. The command targets recently changed files by default rather than the entire codebase. It is most effective when run immediately after implementing a feature, before committing. The command produces a unified diff of its suggested changes for your review before applying. You can accept, reject, or further refine the suggestions interactively.

features.name: /goal
features.summary_ja: 完了条件を設定して複数ターンを自動実行
features.description_ja: 完了条件をClaudeに設定し、条件が満たされるまで複数ターンにわたって自動実行する（v2.1.139追加）。インタラクティブ・--print・Remote Control のすべてで動作。実行中は経過時間・ターン数・トークン数をオーバーレイパネルでリアルタイム表示。

examples.title: Run until condition met
examples.code: /goal テストがすべて通るまで修正を続けて

features.summary_en: Set a completion condition and run multiple turns automatically until Claude satisfies it, with a real-time progress overlay; Sets a completion condition for Claude and runs automatically across multiple turns.
features.description_en: Sets a completion condition for Claude and runs automatically across multiple turns until the condition is satisfied (added in v2.1.139). Works in interactive, --print, and Remote Control modes. An overlay panel shows elapsed time, turn count, and token usage in real time. Goals can be simple conditions like 'all tests pass' or complex multi-step objectives. Claude re-evaluates the condition after each turn and stops automatically when satisfied. Goals that require tool access work best when combined with acceptEdits mode so Claude can make changes without interrupting the loop to ask for permission.

features.name: /scroll-speed
features.summary_ja: マウスホイールのスクロール速度を調整
features.description_ja: マウスホイールのスクロール速度をリアルタイムプレビュー付きで調整するコマンド（v2.1.139追加）。カーソルや VS Code でのスクロール速度問題にも対応しており、トラックパッドとマウスホイールのスクロール量を別々に制御できる。
features.summary_en: Adjust mouse wheel scroll speed with a real-time preview; supports separate controls for trackpad and mouse wheel; Adjusts mouse wheel scroll speed with a real-time preview (added in v2.1.139).
features.description_en: Adjusts mouse wheel scroll speed with a real-time preview (added in v2.1.139). Addresses scroll speed issues in Cursor and VS Code environments. Trackpad and mouse wheel scroll amounts can be controlled separately. The preview lets you test the feel immediately without saving, so you can iterate quickly. Settings are stored in ~/.claude/settings.json under the scrollSpeed key. Scroll speed settings apply only to mouse wheel events in the Claude Code TUI. Terminal emulator scroll settings are separate and must be configured in your terminal preferences.

features.name: /schedule
features.summary_ja: 定期スケジュールのリモートエージェントを管理
features.description_ja: cron スケジュールで定期実行するリモートエージェント（ルーティン）の作成・一覧・実行を管理する（v2.1.139）。claude.ai 認証が必要で、ANTHROPIC_API_KEY 設定時は Remote Control・claude.ai MCP コネクタと同様に無効化される。
features.summary_en: Create, list, and run remote agents on a cron schedule; requires claude.ai authentication and is disabled with API key auth.
features.description_en: Creates, lists, and runs remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule (v2.1.139). Requires claude.ai authentication; disabled when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set, just like Remote Control and the claude.ai MCP connector. Scheduled routines run as remote agents on claude.ai infrastructure and can be viewed or cancelled from the /schedule dialog. Each routine has its own cron expression and prompt. Routines are serverless: they start a fresh Claude Code session for each run, so they do not accumulate context across invocations. Use CLAUDE.md to provide persistent instructions.

features.name: /ultraplan
features.summary_ja: クラウドセッションで計画をリファイン
features.description_ja: プランモードから「Refine with Ultraplan」として起動できるクラウドベースの計画精緻化機能（v2.1.101追加）。デフォルトのクラウド環境を自動生成してリモートセッションURLをトランスクリプトに表示。組織またはAuth設定でクラウドに到達できない場合は非表示になる。
features.summary_en: Refine a plan using a cloud-based session launched from plan mode as the Refine with Ultraplan option; A cloud-based plan refinement feature accessible from plan mode as 'Refine with Ultraplan'.
features.description_en: A cloud-based plan refinement feature accessible from plan mode as 'Refine with Ultraplan' (added in v2.1.101). Automatically creates a default cloud environment and displays the remote session URL in the transcript. Hidden when the cloud is unreachable due to organization or auth settings. Ultraplan creates a cloud session that has access to additional compute for longer-horizon planning. The resulting plan is returned to your local session as a structured document. The Ultraplan session has internet access and can browse documentation to produce more accurate plans. The resulting plan document is saved locally and linked in your transcript.

features.name: /powerup
features.summary_ja: インタラクティブな機能チュートリアルを起動
features.description_ja: Claude Code の各機能をアニメーションデモ付きのインタラクティブなレッスン形式で学べる（v2.1.90追加）。ハンズオン形式で機能の使い方を習得できる。
features.summary_en: Launch interactive feature tutorials with animated demos to learn Claude Code capabilities hands-on; Lets you learn Claude Code features through interactive, animated lessons (added in v2.1.90).
features.description_en: Lets you learn Claude Code features through interactive, animated lessons (added in v2.1.90). Hands-on format helps you build familiarity with features quickly. A great starting point for new users or when exploring features you have not used before. Lessons cover features from basic slash commands to advanced hooks and multi-agent workflows. Progress is tracked so you can resume the tutorial across sessions. Lessons can be paused and resumed at any time. Progress is saved so you can complete the tutorial across multiple sessions without losing track of where you left off.

features.name: /teleport
features.summary_ja: リモートセッションを再開（claude.ai登録者向け）
features.description_ja: claude.ai 登録者向けのリモートセッション再開コマンド（v2.1.0追加）。/remote-env でリモートセッションを設定し、/teleport で再開する。Remote Control との組み合わせで利用可能。
features.summary_en: Resume a remote session for claude.ai subscribers; configure the target environment first with /remote-env; Resumes a remote session for claude.ai subscribers (added in v2.1.0).
features.description_en: Resumes a remote session for claude.ai subscribers (added in v2.1.0). Configure the remote session first with /remote-env, then use /teleport to reconnect. Can be combined with Remote Control for a seamless cross-device workflow. Teleporting reconnects to the most recent remote session associated with your account. If multiple remote sessions exist, a picker is shown to select the correct one. Teleporting preserves your full session context including tool call history. The remote session appears in the claude.ai web interface exactly as it was in your local terminal.

features.name: /remote-env
features.summary_ja: リモートセッション環境を設定（claude.ai登録者向け）
features.description_ja: claude.ai 登録者向けのリモートセッション設定コマンド（v2.1.0追加）。リモート環境のパラメータを設定し、/teleport でセッションを再開する際の接続先を定義する。
features.summary_en: Configure the remote session environment parameters for claude.ai subscribers used when reconnecting via /teleport; Configures the remote session environment for claude.ai subscribers (added in v2.1.0).
features.description_en: Configures the remote session environment for claude.ai subscribers (added in v2.1.0). Sets the connection parameters for the remote environment used when reconnecting with /teleport. Remote environment parameters include compute size, region, and timeout settings. Changes made here persist and are used automatically the next time you run /teleport. Remote environment parameters are stored encrypted in your claude.ai account. They are not saved to local settings files, so they are available on any device you log in from. Both /remote-env and /teleport require an active claude.ai subscription.

features.name: /bg
features.summary_ja: バックグラウンドエージェントを起動
features.description_ja: バックグラウンドエージェントをコマンドまたはキーボードショートカット ←← で起動する。起動時に現在の権限モードを引き継ぐ（v2.1.141）。実行中のタスクは /tasks で確認可能。
features.summary_en: Launch a background agent via command or the double left-arrow keyboard shortcut, inheriting the current permission mode; Launches a background agent via command or the ←← keyboard shortcut.
features.description_en: Launches a background agent via command or the ←← keyboard shortcut. Inherits the current permission mode at launch (v2.1.141). Running tasks can be monitored with /tasks. Background agents use the same tools as foreground sessions but run autonomously without interactive input. Monitor their progress and retrieve results via the /tasks dialog. Background agents run in a separate process from your main session and do not block the main session from accepting new prompts. You can start multiple background agents simultaneously.

features.name: /setup-vertex
features.summary_ja: Google Vertex AI接続を対話形式でセットアップ
features.description_ja: Google Cloud Vertex AI 経由でClaudeを利用するための設定を対話形式で実施（v2.1.111改善）。CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR 設定時は実際のパスを表示し、既存ピンからモデル候補を提案、対応モデルでは1Mコンテキストオプションも提示。
features.summary_en: Interactively configure Google Cloud Vertex AI as the Claude provider with model suggestions and 1M context options; Interactively sets up Claude usage via Google Cloud Vertex AI (improved in v2.1.111).
features.description_en: Interactively sets up Claude usage via Google Cloud Vertex AI (improved in v2.1.111). Displays actual paths when CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is set, proposes model candidates from existing pins, and offers a 1M context option for supported models. Similar to /setup-bedrock for AWS. Vertex AI setup requires a valid Google Cloud project with the Vertex AI API enabled. The wizard validates your gcloud credentials and project before saving the configuration. After setup, Claude Code tests the Vertex AI endpoint before saving the configuration. If the test fails, check that the Vertex AI API is enabled in your Google Cloud project.

features.name: /chrome
features.summary_ja: ブラウザをClaudeから直接操作（Beta）
features.description_ja: Claude in Chrome拡張機能（claude.ai/chrome）と連携し、ブラウザを直接Claude Codeから操作できるベータ機能（v2.0.72追加）。認証設定によっては非表示になる場合がある。
features.summary_en: Control the browser directly from Claude Code using the Claude in Chrome extension as a beta feature; Integrates with the Claude in Chrome extension (claude.ai/chrome) to control the browser directly.
features.description_en: Integrates with the Claude in Chrome extension (claude.ai/chrome) to control the browser directly from Claude Code (Beta, added in v2.0.72). May be hidden depending on authentication configuration. Useful for web automation and testing workflows. The Claude in Chrome extension must be installed and connected before this command is available. Once connected, Claude can read page content, click elements, and fill forms directly. The Chrome extension communicates with Claude Code via a local WebSocket connection. Both the extension and Claude Code must be running on the same machine for the integration to work.

features.name: /commit-push-pr
features.summary_ja: コミット・プッシュ・PR作成を一括実行
features.description_ja: git commit・push・PR作成を一括実行するバンドルスキル。MCP経由でSlackが設定されている場合はPR URLを自動的にSlackチャンネルに投稿する（v2.1.20で変更）。
features.summary_en: Run git commit, push, and PR creation as a single bundled command; posts PR URL to Slack if MCP is configured.
features.description_en: Runs git commit, push, and PR creation in one bundled skill command. When Slack is configured via MCP, the PR URL is automatically posted to a Slack channel (changed in v2.1.20). Streamlines the end-of-task workflow. The skill generates a commit message from the diff, pushes to the current remote, and opens a PR with an AI-generated description. Review each step before confirming. You can customize the commit message and PR title during the skill execution. The Slack notification includes the PR URL and title, making it easy for teammates to start reviewing immediately.

features.name: /web-setup
features.summary_ja: GitHub App Web接続をセットアップ
features.description_ja: Claude Code とGitHub AppのWeb接続を設定するダイアログ（v2.1.136追加）。既存の接続を上書きする前に警告を表示する（v2.1.142）。Escキーでダイアログを閉じられる。
features.summary_en: Configure the GitHub App web connection for Claude Code; warns before overwriting an existing connection; Configures the web connection between Claude Code and the GitHub App (added in v2.1.136).
features.description_en: Configures the web connection between Claude Code and the GitHub App (added in v2.1.136). Warns before overwriting an existing connection (v2.1.142). The dialog can be closed with Esc. The web connection allows GitHub to send events to Claude Code for PR-based workflows. Only one web connection can be active at a time; the warning prevents accidental overwrites. The web connection uses a webhook to receive GitHub events. Store the webhook secret securely; the /web-setup dialog guides you through entering it without displaying it in the transcript.

features.name: /update
features.summary_ja: Claude Codeをセッション内から最新版に更新
features.description_ja: セッション内から Claude Code を最新バージョンに更新するコマンド。`claude update` CLI サブコマンドと同等の更新を対話セッションから実行可能。worktree 内セッションでも動作（v2.1.116 修正）。`DISABLE_UPDATES` 環境変数で全更新パスを無効化可能。
features.summary_en: Update Claude Code to the latest version from within an active session, equivalent to running claude update in the CLI.
features.description_en: Updates Claude Code to the latest version from within an interactive session. Equivalent to the claude update CLI subcommand. Works in worktree sessions too (fixed in v2.1.116). Set the DISABLE_UPDATES environment variable to disable all update paths. Claude Code checks for updates on every launch, but /update lets you apply them immediately without restarting. The DISABLE_UPDATES variable is useful in managed enterprise environments. Update progress is displayed inline in the session. The updated version takes effect on the next session start; the current session continues running on the old version until then.

features.name: /fork
features.summary_ja: /branch の旧コマンド名（エイリアス）
features.description_ja: v2.1.77 で `/branch` にリネームされた旧コマンド名。エイリアスとして引き続き動作し、セッションをフォークして別の方向を試すことができる。分岐したセッションは /resume で再開可能。新しい記法では `/branch` を使用することを推奨。
features.summary_en: Legacy alias for /branch that forks the session to try a different direction; new usage should prefer /branch; The old command name renamed to /branch in v2.1.77.
features.description_en: The old command name renamed to /branch in v2.1.77. Continues to work as an alias and forks the session to try a different direction. Branched sessions can be resumed with /resume. New usage should prefer /branch. The /fork alias is retained for backward compatibility with existing scripts and documentation. Functionally identical to /branch, it creates an independent copy of the current session state. The /fork command was renamed because forking in a git workflow typically means something else. Using /branch makes the session-branching behavior clearer and avoids naming confusion.

features.name: /batch
features.summary_ja: バッチ操作を一括実行するバンドル型スラッシュコマンド
features.description_ja: v2.1.63 で /simplify と同時に追加されたバンドル型スラッシュコマンド。CHANGELOG には「Added /simplify and /batch bundled slash commands」の一行のみ記載。詳細は /release-notes で v2.1.63 のリリースノートを参照。
features.summary_en: Run batch operations as a bundled slash command added alongside /simplify in v2.1.63; see /release-notes for details; A bundled slash command added in v2.1.63 alongside /simplify.
features.description_en: A bundled slash command added in v2.1.63 alongside /simplify. The CHANGELOG notes only 'Added /simplify and /batch bundled slash commands'. For full details, see the v2.1.63 release notes via /release-notes. The exact behavior of /batch is not fully documented in the changelog. Use /release-notes and select v2.1.63 from the picker to read the original release note describing this command. If you need to understand exactly what /batch does, inspect the bundled skill definition after installing the plugin that contains it, or review the v2.1.63 changelog entry.

features.name: /usage-credits
features.summary_ja: 追加API利用（使用クレジット）を有効化
features.description_ja: プランの使用量上限を超えた追加API利用（使用クレジット）を有効化するダイアログを開く。v2.1.144 で「extra usage」から「usage credits」に名称変更され、/extra-usage から /usage-credits にリネーム。旧コマンド名 /extra-usage も引き続き動作する。
features.summary_en: Open a dialog to enable usage credits for additional API usage beyond the plan limit; renamed from /extra-usage in v2.1.144.
features.description_en: Opens a dialog to enable usage credits for additional API usage beyond your plan limit. Renamed from 'extra usage' to 'usage credits' in v2.1.144; the old command /extra-usage continues to work as an alias. Usage credits appear as a separate line item on your Anthropic invoice. The /usage dialog shows current credit balance and consumption alongside your regular plan usage. Usage credits are separate from your subscription plan allowance. They provide a pay-as-you-go buffer that prevents interruptions when your monthly plan usage is exhausted.

features.name: /heapdump
features.summary_ja: ヒープメモリダンプを生成する開発者向けコマンド
features.description_ja: デバッグ用に Node.js ヒープメモリダンプファイルを生成する。メモリ使用量の問題調査やバグ報告時に使用する開発者向け診断コマンド。Windows では v2.1.73 でデスクトップフォルダが既存の場合の EEXIST エラーを修正済み。
features.summary_en: Generate a Node.js heap memory dump file for diagnosing memory usage issues or preparing detailed bug reports; Generates a Node.js heap memory dump file for debugging.
features.description_en: Generates a Node.js heap memory dump file for debugging. A developer diagnostic command for investigating memory usage issues or preparing detailed bug reports. On Windows, an EEXIST error when the desktop folder already exists was fixed in v2.1.73. The dump file is saved to the desktop by default and can be analyzed with Chrome DevTools or the v8-profiler library. Include the file when filing memory-related bug reports. Heap dumps can be large files (100MB or more). Share them only with Anthropic support or via a secure channel, as they may contain snippets of code or file paths from your project.

features.name: /code-review
features.summary_ja: コードレビューと修正を一括実行（/simplifyの改名版）
features.description_ja: v2.1.146で/simplifyから改名。変更されたコードを再利用性・品質・効率の観点でレビューし、検出した問題を修正する。オプションのeffort levelで処理強度を指定可能（例: /code-review high）。
features.summary_en: Review and fix changed code for quality and efficiency with an optional effort level; renamed from /simplify in v2.1.146; Renamed from /simplify in v2.1.146.
features.description_en: Renamed from /simplify in v2.1.146. Reviews changed code for reusability, quality, and efficiency, then fixes detected issues. Accepts an optional effort level argument to control review depth (e.g., /code-review high). Effort levels map to the same scale as /effort: low for a quick scan, high for deep analysis. The default effort is equivalent to normal, balancing speed and thoroughness.
