With high effort, the review examines edge cases, algorithmic complexity, and subtle logic errors that lower effort might miss. Use it for critical code paths before shipping to production.

features.name: /env
features.summary_ja: ツール実行環境の環境変数を設定・管理
features.description_ja: Bash・PowerShell ツールが使用する環境変数をセッション内で動的に設定・確認するコマンド。v2.1.89 以降、Bash だけでなく PowerShell ツールにも適用される。settings.json の env: 設定と組み合わせて使用できる。
features.summary_en: Set and manage environment variables used by Bash and PowerShell tools dynamically within the current session; Dynamically sets and inspects environment variables used by Bash and PowerShell tools within the.
features.description_en: Dynamically sets and inspects environment variables used by Bash and PowerShell tools within the session. Since v2.1.89 it applies to PowerShell as well as Bash. Can be combined with the env: setting in settings.json for persistent defaults. Environment variables set here override both the process environment and any env: values in settings.json for the duration of the session. They are not persisted after the session ends. Use /env to inject secrets or feature flags for a single session without modifying global environment files. Variables set here are visible only to tools running within this session.

features.name: /ide
features.summary_ja: IDE拡張機能を自動インストール
features.description_ja: VS Code や JetBrains などの IDE 拡張機能をコマンドから自動インストールする。IDE 内で Claude Code を利用するための拡張機能のオンボーディングを自動化。インストール処理のエラーは v2.1.73 で修正済み。
features.summary_en: Automatically install VS Code or JetBrains IDE extensions for Claude Code integration from within the session; Automatically installs IDE extensions for VS Code, JetBrains, and others from the command line.
features.description_en: Automatically installs IDE extensions for VS Code, JetBrains, and others from the command line. Automates the extension onboarding process for using Claude Code inside an IDE. Installation error handling was fixed in v2.1.73. The /ide command detects your current IDE automatically. If detection fails, you can specify the IDE explicitly. Existing extensions are updated rather than reinstalled. After installation the IDE extension connects automatically on next editor launch. If the connection does not establish, check that both Claude Code and the IDE extension are on compatible versions.

features.name: /diff
features.summary_ja: コード変更の差分詳細ビューを表示
features.description_ja: 差分（diff）詳細ビューを開き、コード変更内容を確認する。矢印キー・j/k・PgUp/PgDn・Space・Home/End でスクロール可能（v2.1.149 でキーボードナビゲーションが追加）。
features.summary_en: Open a detailed diff view to inspect code changes with keyboard navigation; scroll with arrow keys, j/k, PgUp/PgDn, Space, and Home/End.
features.description_en: Opens a detailed diff view to inspect current code changes inline. Keyboard navigation was added in v2.1.149: use arrow keys, j/k, PgUp/PgDn, Space, and Home/End to scroll through the diff. Useful for reviewing what Claude has changed before committing or rewinding. The diff view shows all modified files in a unified format with syntax highlighting. Navigate between files and hunks using the keyboard shortcuts. Pair with /review to get both a visual diff and a quality assessment of your changes. Use /rewind if you want to roll back a specific change after reviewing it here.

features.name: /mobile
features.summary_ja: モバイルアプリ連携の設定メニューを開く
features.description_ja: Remote Control のモバイル接続設定などを管理するメニューコマンド。/voice・/chrome・/upgrade と同様に認証設定によって非表示になる（v2.1.84）。メニューのレイアウト・スペーシングは v2.1.147 で修正済み。
features.summary_en: Open the mobile app connection settings menu for Remote Control integration.
features.description_en: A menu command for managing mobile connection settings in Remote Control. Similar to /voice, /chrome, and /upgrade, this command is hidden based on authentication configuration (since v2.1.84). The menu layout and spacing were fixed in v2.1.147. Use this command to configure and manage your mobile device pairing and remote control preferences within Claude Code.

features.name: /plugins
features.summary_ja: プラグイン一覧とマーケットプレイス探索ビューを開く
features.description_ja: インストール済みプラグインの Installed タブとマーケットプレイスから新プラグインを探す discover ビューを持つプラグインブラウザ。v2.1.2 で Installed タブがプラグインと MCP をスコープ別グループ化に統合（v2.0.74 で discover ビューの表示バグ修正）。管理操作は /plugin も参照。
features.summary_en: Open the plugin browser with an Installed tab and a discover view for exploring new plugins from the Marketplace.
features.description_en: A plugin browser with an Installed tab showing current plugins and a discover view for finding new plugins from the Marketplace. Since v2.1.2 the Installed tab groups plugins and MCP servers by scope. A display bug in the discover view was fixed in v2.0.74. For management operations such as install, uninstall, and update, use /plugin. The discover view lets you browse Marketplace plugins by category and install them directly without leaving Claude Code. Use /plugins discover to jump straight to the explore view when you know you want to add something new.

features.name: /background
features.summary_ja: バックグラウンドセッションを起動（/bg のフルネーム）
features.description_ja: /bg コマンドのフルネーム。バックグラウンドでエージェントセッションを起動する。v2.1.147 でスキルやカスタムスラッシュコマンドのみが入力された場合にセッションを拒否するバグが修正された。実行中タスクは /tasks で確認可能。
features.summary_en: Launch a background agent session; the full name equivalent of /bg with a bug fix for skill-only inputs in v2.1.147.
features.description_en: The full-name equivalent of /bg. Launches an agent session in the background. A bug where sessions were rejected when only skills or custom slash commands were provided as input was fixed in v2.1.147. Running tasks can be monitored with /tasks. Background agents run independently of the main session and do not block interactive use. They inherit the permission mode active at launch. Use /tasks to view progress, retrieve results, and manage all running background sessions from one place.

features.name: Bash
features.summary_ja: シェルコマンドを実行
features.description_ja: シェル環境でコマンドを実行。npm install、git操作、ビルドコマンドなどに使う。最大10分のタイムアウト指定可能。
features.summary_en: Execute shell commands with a configurable timeout of up to ten minutes for npm, git, builds, and any shell task.
features.description_en: Executes commands in the shell environment. Used for npm install, git operations, build commands, and any other shell-level task. A timeout of up to 10 minutes can be specified per invocation. The most fundamental tool for interacting with the local system. Background execution is supported via the run_in_background parameter, which frees the session to continue while the command runs. Output is streamed back when the command completes. The command's working directory defaults to the session's cwd. Use cd only when needed; absolute paths are generally more reliable in hook scripts and automation.

features.name: Read
features.summary_ja: ファイルを読み取り
features.description_ja: ファイルの内容を読み取る。テキスト、PDF、画像、Jupyter notebookに対応。行番号付き、オフセット指定可能。
features.summary_en: Read file contents with line numbers and offset support for text, PDF, images, and Jupyter notebooks; Reads the contents of a file with line numbers.
features.description_en: Reads the contents of a file with line numbers. Supports text files, PDFs, images, and Jupyter notebooks. Supports offset-based reading for large files. Always use Read before Edit or Write to ensure you have the latest file content. For large files, provide offset and limit parameters to read only the relevant section. Reading PDFs requires a pages parameter for files longer than ten pages. When reading code files, Claude uses line numbers to reference specific lines in follow-up edits. Providing offset and limit keeps reads targeted and conserves context budget.

features.name: Write
features.summary_ja: 新規ファイルを作成
features.description_ja: ローカルファイルシステムにファイルを作成・上書き。既存ファイルは事前に Read 必須。

examples.title: Create new file
examples.code: Write: 新ファイルを書き出す

features.summary_en: Create or fully overwrite a file on the local filesystem; a prior Read is required for existing files; Creates or overwrites a file on the local filesystem.
features.description_en: Creates or overwrites a file on the local filesystem. For existing files, a prior Read is required. Prefer Edit for modifying existing files since it only sends the diff. Use Write for new files or when replacing the entire contents. Write overwrites the entire file, so it is best for new files or complete rewrites. For surgical changes to existing files, prefer Edit or MultiEdit to minimize diff noise. Write creates parent directories automatically if they do not exist. After writing, Claude can immediately read the file back to verify the content without an extra Read call.

features.name: Edit
features.summary_ja: 既存ファイルを部分編集
features.description_ja: old_string と new_string を指定して文字列置換。差分のみ送信できるので Write より効率的。
features.summary_en: Perform targeted string replacement in an existing file by specifying old_string and new_string; more efficient than Write; Performs string replacement by specifying old_string and new_string.
features.description_en: Performs string replacement by specifying old_string and new_string. More efficient than Write because only the diff is transmitted. Use for targeted modifications to existing files. Fails if old_string is not unique; provide more surrounding context in that case. If old_string appears more than once, the edit fails with an ambiguity error. Provide a larger context string that makes the match unique, or use the replace_all option. Multiple sequential Edit calls on the same file are efficient because each one only transmits the changed portion. For several changes at once, MultiEdit is even faster.

features.name: MultiEdit
features.summary_ja: 1ファイルに複数編集を一括適用
features.description_ja: 複数の Edit 操作を同時にトランザクションで適用。1つでも失敗すると全てロールバック。
features.summary_en: Apply multiple edits to a single file as one atomic transaction; all edits roll back if any one fails; Applies multiple Edit operations to a single file as a single.
features.description_en: Applies multiple Edit operations to a single file as a single transaction. If any one edit fails, all edits are rolled back. Use this when multiple changes must succeed together to keep the file in a consistent state. All edits in a MultiEdit call are applied in order before any are validated, so specify changes in the correct sequence. Rollback is automatic on any failure in the batch. MultiEdit is the preferred tool when refactoring requires coordinated changes, such as renaming a variable in multiple locations while also updating an import statement.

features.name: Glob
features.summary_ja: パターンでファイル検索
features.description_ja: ワイルドカードパターン（src/**/*.ts等）でファイルを高速検索。
features.summary_en: Search for files matching wildcard patterns such as src/**/*.ts for fast file discovery across large codebases; Searches for files using wildcard patterns (e.g., src/**/*.ts).
features.description_en: Searches for files using wildcard patterns (e.g., src/**/*.ts). Fast and suitable for discovering files by name or extension across large codebases. Use Grep when you need to search by file contents rather than file names. Glob is significantly faster than Bash find for file discovery because it uses the native filesystem index. Combine with Grep to first locate files by name then search their contents. Glob patterns are anchored to the working directory by default. Start with ** to search recursively or restrict to a subdirectory prefix to narrow the search scope.

features.name: Grep
features.summary_ja: ファイル内容を全文検索
features.description_ja: ripgrepベースの高速検索。正規表現対応、コンテキスト行表示、ファイル種別フィルタなど多機能。
features.summary_en: Full-text search file contents using ripgrep with regex support, context lines, and file-type filtering; Ripgrep-based high-speed content search; Supports regular expressions, context line display, and file-type filtering.
features.description_en: Ripgrep-based high-speed content search. Supports regular expressions, context line display, and file-type filtering. The go-to tool for finding function definitions, variable usages, or any string pattern across the codebase. Ripgrep respects .gitignore files by default, skipping build artifacts and vendor directories automatically. Override with the --no-ignore flag when you need to search ignored files. Output includes file path, line number, and matching line text for easy navigation. Use the context flag to show surrounding lines when you need to understand the match in context.

features.name: Task (Agent)
features.summary_ja: サブエージェントを起動
features.description_ja: 別のエージェントを起動して並列タスク実行。Explore/Plan/general-purpose 等の組み込みエージェントを使える。
features.summary_en: Launch a subagent in a separate context to execute tasks in parallel using built-in or custom agent types; Launches another agent to execute tasks in parallel.
features.description_en: Launches another agent to execute tasks in parallel. Built-in agent types include Explore, Plan, and general-purpose. Subagents operate in separate contexts, so parallel execution does not consume the main session's context window. Subagents inherit a copy of the parent context at launch but run independently afterward. Results are returned to the parent session when the subagent completes its task. Task results are returned as text to the parent session. For structured data, instruct the subagent to output JSON so the parent session can parse and use the results programmatically.

features.name: WebSearch
features.summary_ja: Webを検索
features.description_ja: 最新情報を検索。日付・ドメインフィルタ対応。米国限定。
features.summary_en: Search the web for up-to-date information with optional date and domain filters; currently limited to US results; Searches the web for up-to-date information.
features.description_en: Searches the web for up-to-date information. Supports date and domain filters. Currently limited to US-based results. Use for finding recent documentation, changelogs, or information that may not be in the model's training data. Search results include titles, URLs, and snippets rather than full page content. Follow up with WebFetch on specific URLs to retrieve the complete page when more detail is needed. Search queries support advanced operators like site: and after: for date filtering. Combine with WebFetch on the most relevant result URL to get the full page content.

features.name: WebFetch
features.summary_ja: URLを取得して要約
features.description_ja: 指定URLからHTML取得→マークダウン変換→AIが要約。15分キャッシュあり。
features.summary_en: Fetch a URL, convert the HTML to markdown, and return an AI-generated summary; results are cached for fifteen minutes; Fetches HTML from a specified URL, converts it to markdown, and.
features.description_en: Fetches HTML from a specified URL, converts it to markdown, and returns an AI-generated summary. Results are cached for 15 minutes. Use for reading documentation pages, API references, or any web content that Claude needs as context. WebFetch is ideal for reading API documentation, GitHub issues, and blog posts. The 15-minute cache means repeated fetches of the same URL are fast and free during a session. WebFetch handles JavaScript-rendered pages by running a headless browser under the hood. This makes it reliable for fetching documentation from modern single-page application sites.

features.name: NotebookEdit
features.summary_ja: Jupyter Notebookセルを編集
features.description_ja: .ipynb ファイルの特定セルを編集・追加・削除。データサイエンス作業向け。
features.summary_en: Edit, add, or delete specific cells in a Jupyter Notebook .ipynb file for data science workflows; Edits, adds, or deletes specific cells in a .ipynb file.
features.description_en: Edits, adds, or deletes specific cells in a .ipynb file. Designed for data science workflows where Jupyter Notebooks are the primary artifact. Use this instead of Edit/Write for notebook files to preserve the cell structure correctly. Use NotebookEdit to add new cells, replace cell source code, or remove cells by ID. Output cells are read-only and cannot be edited directly; re-run the cell to update them. Cell IDs are assigned by Jupyter and visible in the raw .ipynb JSON. When adding a new cell, specify the position relative to an existing cell ID to control insertion order.

features.name: TodoWrite
features.summary_ja: タスクリストで進捗管理
features.description_ja: 現在のセッション内のタスク追跡。pending/in_progress/completed の3状態。複雑な作業の整理に。

examples.title: Add tasks
examples.code: TodoWrite: 認証実装、テスト追加

features.summary_en: Track session task progress with pending, in-progress, and completed states; view all items via the /todos overlay; Tracks tasks within the current session with three states: pending, in-progress, and completed.
features.description_en: Tracks tasks within the current session with three states: pending, in-progress, and completed. Useful for organizing complex multi-step work. The /todos command shows an overlay of all current items without scrolling through the conversation. Claude uses TodoWrite autonomously to track progress on multi-step tasks without being asked. You can read the current list at any time with the /todos command. Best practice is to create the full task list at the start of a complex request and mark items in-progress and completed as Claude works through them. This gives users clear progress visibility.

features.name: SlashCommand
features.summary_ja: 別のスラッシュコマンドを実行
features.description_ja: Claude が他のスラッシュコマンドを呼び出す内部ツール。コマンド連携の基盤。
features.summary_en: Internal tool that allows Claude to invoke other slash commands programmatically as the foundation for command chaining; An internal tool that allows Claude to call other slash commands programmatically.
features.description_en: An internal tool that allows Claude to call other slash commands programmatically. Serves as the foundation for command chaining. Not typically invoked directly by users; it is used under the hood by bundled slash commands and skills. Skills and bundled commands rely on SlashCommand to chain multiple commands together. Understanding this tool is useful when debugging skill behavior or building complex automations. The SlashCommand tool passes arguments to the target command in the same way a user would type them. Skills that invoke other skills use this tool to chain behavior.

features.name: PreToolUse Hook
features.summary_ja: ツール実行前のフック
features.description_ja: ツール実行直前に走るフック。matcher で対象ツール指定、危険コマンドのブロックや事前検証に使える。
features.summary_en: Hook that runs before a tool executes to validate input, block dangerous commands, or perform pre-execution checks; A hook that runs immediately before a tool executes.
features.description_en: A hook that runs immediately before a tool executes. Use a matcher to target specific tools. Commonly used to block dangerous commands like rm -rf or to perform pre-execution validation. The hook can return a block decision to prevent execution. The hook receives the full tool input as JSON and can modify it before execution or return a block response to prevent the tool from running entirely. Return a JSON object with block: true and a reason string to prevent execution. Claude will report the block reason to the user so they understand why the action was prevented.

features.name: PostToolUse Hook
features.summary_ja: ツール実行後のフック
features.description_ja: ツール実行直後に走るフック。ログ記録、自動フォーマット、テスト実行などに使う。
features.summary_en: Hook that runs after a tool executes for logging, automatic code formatting, test execution, or other post-action automation; A hook that runs immediately after a tool executes.
features.description_en: A hook that runs immediately after a tool executes. Used for logging, auto-formatting, running tests, or any post-action automation. For example, automatically run prettier after every Edit or Write operation. The hook receives both the tool input and the result, enabling conditional logic based on what the tool actually did. Use it to trigger tests only when relevant files were changed. The hook is non-blocking by default: it runs asynchronously after the tool completes and does not delay the session. Use it for fire-and-forget side effects like logging.

features.name: Stop Hook
features.summary_ja: Claude応答完了時のフック
features.description_ja: Claudeの応答が終了したときに走るフック。完了通知やセッションログ出力に。
features.summary_en: Hook that fires when Claude finishes a response, used for completion sounds, desktop notifications, or session log output; A hook that runs when Claude's response finishes.
features.description_en: A hook that runs when Claude's response finishes. Used for completion notifications and session log output. Ideal for playing a sound, sending a desktop notification, or writing a summary when Claude is done. The Stop hook fires after every Claude response, including intermediate responses during a multi-turn task. Filter by session state if you only want notifications at true completion. Hooks that take longer than a few seconds to run will delay the session noticeably. Keep Stop hooks lightweight; offload heavy work to background processes launched by the hook.

features.name: SubagentStop Hook
features.summary_ja: サブエージェント完了時のフック
features.description_ja: サブエージェント（Task tool）が完了したときに走るフック。
features.summary_en: Hook that fires when a subagent launched via the Task tool completes its task, enabling downstream automation; A hook that runs when a subagent (Task tool) completes.
features.description_en: A hook that runs when a subagent (Task tool) completes. Use it to log completion events, aggregate results from parallel agents, or trigger downstream actions once a specific subagent finishes its work. The SubagentStop hook receives the subagent ID and result so you can route different outcomes to different downstream processes based on which agent finished. Subagent IDs are stable within a session, so you can map results back to the original Task call that launched each subagent. Log both IDs to make debugging parallel runs easier.

features.name: SessionStart Hook
features.summary_ja: セッション開始時のフック
features.description_ja: Claude Code セッション開始時に走るフック。git pull や初期メッセージ表示に。
features.summary_en: Hook that runs when a Claude Code session starts, suitable for auto git pull or displaying an initial setup message.
features.description_en: A hook that runs when a Claude Code session starts. Use for running git pull to ensure a fresh codebase, displaying an initial message, or performing any setup required before work begins. The SessionStart hook fires once per session, making it ideal for expensive setup operations like pulling dependencies or generating a context summary for Claude to read. The SessionStart hook runs before any user prompt is processed. Use it to set environment variables, validate prerequisites, or populate context that every session needs.

features.name: SessionEnd Hook
features.summary_ja: セッション終了時のフック
features.description_ja: セッション終了時に走るフック。コミット忘れ警告、サマリー出力など。
features.summary_en: Hook that runs when a session ends, used for uncommitted change warnings, summary output, or cleanup tasks; A hook that runs when a session ends.
features.description_en: A hook that runs when a session ends. Used for uncommitted change warnings, summary output, or cleanup tasks. Complements SessionStart for bracketing a session with setup and teardown logic. The SessionEnd hook fires even when the session exits abnormally, making it reliable for cleanup tasks. Avoid long-running operations here as they can delay session teardown. Data written by the SessionEnd hook is available for the next SessionStart hook to read. This pattern enables persistent cross-session state without using the built-in memory system.

features.name: UserPromptSubmit Hook
features.summary_ja: ユーザー入力送信時のフック
features.description_ja: ユーザーがプロンプトを送信した直後に走る。入力内容を加工・追記したり、追加コンテキスト注入に使える。

examples.title: Auto-append translation hint
examples.code: UserPromptSubmit: 日本語入力に英訳ヒント追加

features.summary_en: Hook that fires when the user submits a prompt, enabling input modification, context injection, or pre-processing; A hook that runs right after the user submits a prompt.
features.description_en: A hook that runs right after the user submits a prompt. Can modify or append to the input, or inject additional context. For example, automatically append a translation hint to Japanese input before Claude processes it. The hook can inject system-level context such as environment state, current git branch, or user preferences into every prompt automatically, without the user needing to type them. Avoid making the hook too slow, as every prompt submission will be delayed by the hook's execution time. Aim for sub-100ms execution for hooks that modify prompts.

features.name: PreCompact Hook
features.summary_ja: コンテキスト圧縮直前のフック
features.description_ja: コンテキスト圧縮が走る直前に呼ばれる。圧縮前に重要な情報を保存したい時に。
features.summary_en: Hook that runs just before context compaction, allowing important data to be saved to a file before the summary; A hook that runs just before context compaction is triggered.
features.description_en: A hook that runs just before context compaction is triggered. Use it to save important information to a file or external store before the conversation history is summarized and truncated. The PreCompact hook receives the current conversation transcript before summarization. Save it to a local file if you need a verbatim archive of the session before compaction runs. The archive created by PreCompact can be referenced in the next session via @-mention to restore important context that was lost during compaction.

features.name: Notification Hook
features.summary_ja: 通知イベントのフック
features.description_ja: Claudeが通知を出すタイミング（権限要求等）で走るフック。デスクトップ通知連携などに。
features.summary_en: Hook that fires on notification events such as permission requests, enabling integration with desktop alert systems; A hook that runs whenever Claude produces a notification event, such as a permission.
features.description_en: A hook that runs whenever Claude produces a notification event, such as a permission request. Use it to integrate with desktop notification systems, send alerts to Slack, or trigger any action when Claude needs user attention. The Notification hook receives the notification message text and type. Use it to filter notifications by severity, routing only urgent permission requests to desktop alerts. The hook receives all notification types, including tool permission requests and informational messages. Filter by notification type to avoid acting on low-priority informational events.

features.name: Plan Mode
features.summary_ja: 読み取り専用の計画モード
features.description_ja: Shift+Tabで切替。コードを書かずに「これからやること」を提案するだけ。実装前のレビューに最適。
features.summary_en: Read-only planning mode toggled with Shift+Tab; Claude proposes next steps without writing any code; Toggle with Shift+Tab; In plan mode Claude only proposes what to do next without writing any.
features.description_en: Toggle with Shift+Tab. In plan mode Claude only proposes what to do next without writing any code. Ideal for reviewing an approach before committing to implementation. Use /plan to activate it directly from the prompt. Plan mode persists until toggled off with Shift+Tab or deactivated via /plan. Claude indicates visually that it is in plan mode so you are always aware of the current state. Plan mode is useful for collaborative review sessions where you want Claude to describe an approach for human sign-off before any code is written. It prevents accidental changes.

features.name: Permission Modes
features.summary_ja: ツール実行時の確認制御
features.description_ja: default(都度確認)、acceptEdits(編集自動)、bypassPermissions(全自動)、plan(読取のみ)の4モード。
features.summary_en: Four modes controlling tool confirmation: default per-action, acceptEdits auto-approve, bypassPermissions full-auto, plan read-only; Four modes control tool confirmation behavior: default (confirm each time), acceptEdits (auto-approve edits), bypassPermissions (fully automatic), and.
features.description_en: Four modes control tool confirmation behavior: default (confirm each time), acceptEdits (auto-approve edits), bypassPermissions (fully automatic), and plan (read-only). Configure in settings.json or switch interactively during a session. Mode changes can be made via /permissions or by editing the permissionMode field in settings.json. acceptEdits is the most popular mode for pair-programming workflows. Enterprise deployments often lock the permission mode in settings.json so all developers use a consistent security posture. Local overrides in settings.local.json can customize it per developer. The bypassPermissions mode is equivalent to using --dangerously-skip-permissions at the CLI and should only be enabled in trusted, sandboxed environments.

features.name: Output Styles
features.summary_ja: 応答スタイルのカスタマイズ
features.description_ja: Claudeの応答口調・形式を調整。簡潔モード、教育モードなどプリセットあり。
features.summary_en: Customize Claude's response tone and format using built-in presets such as concise mode or educational mode; Adjusts Claude's response tone and format.
features.description_en: Adjusts Claude's response tone and format. Built-in presets include concise mode, educational mode, and others. Useful for tailoring Claude's communication style to your current task or personal preference. Output styles can be layered with custom instructions in CLAUDE.md for fine-grained control. The default style balances brevity with completeness suitable for most coding tasks. Output styles can be combined with system prompts in CLAUDE.md to produce highly customized response behavior. The style presets are a starting point; CLAUDE.md provides fine-grained control.

features.name: Headless Mode (--print)
features.summary_ja: 対話なしで一発実行
features.description_ja: --print フラグで対話UIなしに1回だけプロンプトを実行して結果を返す。CIやスクリプト統合に。
features.summary_en: Run a single prompt non-interactively using the --print flag and return output; designed for CI and script integration; The --print flag runs a single prompt without the interactive UI and.
features.description_en: The --print flag runs a single prompt without the interactive UI and returns the result. Designed for CI pipelines and script integration. Combine with --dangerously-skip-permissions for fully automated runs in trusted environments. Headless mode supports chained commands via piping and can be scripted in bash, Python, or any shell. Combine with jq for structured output processing when using the JSON output format. In headless mode, Claude Code exits with code 0 on success and non-zero on error. CI pipelines can use the exit code to gate downstream steps on Claude's success.

features.name: CLAUDE.md
features.summary_ja: プロジェクトガイダンスファイル
features.description_ja: リポジトリ直下に置くと Claude が自動的に読み込むプロジェクトガイダンス。コーディング規約、ディレクトリ構造、注意事項などを書く。
features.summary_en: Project guidance file placed at the repository root that Claude automatically reads for conventions and project context; A project guidance file placed at the repository root that Claude automatically reads.
features.description_en: A project guidance file placed at the repository root that Claude automatically reads at session start. Write coding conventions, directory structure, important warnings, and project-specific instructions here. Generate a template with /init. CLAUDE.md files are hierarchically loaded from parent directories, allowing workspace-level and project-level guidance to coexist. Place the file at the root for project-wide rules. Claude merges CLAUDE.md files from parent directories with the project-level file, so workspace-wide rules in a parent directory automatically apply to all nested projects.

features.name: AGENTS.md
features.summary_ja: エージェント定義ファイル
features.description_ja: サブエージェントの挙動を定義。CLAUDE.md と並列で機能する場合あり。

examples.title: Place file
examples.code: プロジェクトルートに配置

features.summary_en: Agent definition file placed at the project root that configures subagent behavior alongside CLAUDE.md; Defines subagent behavior; May work alongside CLAUDE.md in some configurations.
features.description_en: Defines subagent behavior. May work alongside CLAUDE.md in some configurations. Place at the project root to configure how subagents should operate within this project. AGENTS.md can define multiple named agent types and their specific instruction sets. Entries here take precedence over defaults when Claude selects an agent type for a subagent launch. AGENTS.md entries can reference tool restrictions and scope limits for each agent type. This prevents subagents from accessing resources they do not need for their specific role. Review AGENTS.md alongside CLAUDE.md when onboarding to a new project.

features.name: ONBOARDING.md
features.summary_ja: 新規参加者向けガイド
features.description_ja: プロジェクトに初めて触れる人のためのオンボーディング情報。Claude が読み込んでアシストに使う。

examples.title: Place file
examples.code: プロジェクトルートに配置

features.summary_en: Onboarding guide for new contributors placed at the project root; Claude reads it to assist with ramp-up; Onboarding information for people new to the project.
features.description_en: Onboarding information for people new to the project. Claude reads this file and uses it to assist newcomers. Generate one automatically with /team-onboarding based on local usage history. The file is read alongside CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md at session start. Keep it focused on onboarding-specific content rather than duplicating project rules already in CLAUDE.md. ONBOARDING.md is separate from CLAUDE.md so it can be written for human readers as well as Claude. New team members benefit from the same file whether or not they use Claude Code.

features.name: settings.json
features.summary_ja: ユーザー全体の設定ファイル
features.description_ja: ~/.claude/settings.json でグローバル設定。テーマ、フック、許可リスト、環境変数など。
features.summary_en: Global user settings file at ~/.claude/settings.json for theme, hooks, allowlists, and environment variables; Global settings file at ~/.claude/settings.json; Configures theme, hooks, allowlists, environment variables, and more.
features.description_en: Global settings file at ~/.claude/settings.json. Configures theme, hooks, allowlists, environment variables, and more. Changes here apply to all projects. Use .claude/settings.local.json for project-specific overrides. The file supports hooks, allowlists, environment variables, theme, and model configuration. Changes take effect on the next session start or immediately after a settings reload. Changes to settings.json are applied on the next session start. Use /config for immediate changes in an active session without manually editing the file. The JSON schema for settings.json is documented at code.claude.com and supports comments via the JSONC format.

features.name: settings.local.json
features.summary_ja: プロジェクト固有設定
features.description_ja: .claude/settings.local.json でプロジェクト単位設定。git管理外、個人の許可設定などに。
features.summary_en: Project-specific settings file at .claude/settings.local.json; not tracked by git and suitable for personal overrides; Project-specific settings at .claude/settings.local.json; Not tracked by git, making it suitable for personal permission settings, local.
features.description_en: Project-specific settings at .claude/settings.local.json. Not tracked by git, making it suitable for personal permission settings, local overrides, and developer-specific configuration that should not be shared. Settings in this file take precedence over ~/.claude/settings.json for the current project. Add it to .gitignore to prevent accidentally committing personal access rules. Settings in settings.local.json override both ~/.claude/settings.json and the project settings.json, giving you a clear override hierarchy for multi-layer configuration. The local settings file is created automatically when you save project-level changes via /config or by adding allow rules through /permissions.

features.name: .mcp.json
features.summary_ja: MCPサーバー設定ファイル
features.description_ja: プロジェクトに紐づく MCP サーバーを定義。stdio/SSE/HTTP の各種MCPサーバーを登録できる。
features.summary_en: MCP server configuration file at the project root that registers stdio, SSE, and HTTP servers for Claude to use; Defines MCP servers tied to a project.
features.description_en: Defines MCP servers tied to a project. Supports stdio, SSE, and HTTP server types. Claude loads this automatically for the project. Use --mcp-config to override this file with an external JSON at launch. The .mcp.json file is automatically loaded when Claude Code starts in the project directory. Use --mcp-config at the CLI to override this file with a different configuration. Version-control .mcp.json to share MCP server configuration with your team. Sensitive values like API keys should be stored as environment variable references rather than hardcoded strings.

features.name: Memory System
features.summary_ja: 永続メモリシステム
features.description_ja: ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/memory/ にユーザー嗜好・プロジェクト情報を蓄積。MEMORY.md がインデックス。
features.summary_en: Persistent memory store at ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/memory/ that accumulates preferences and project info across sessions; Stores user preferences and project information persistently at ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/memory/.
features.description_en: Stores user preferences and project information persistently at ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/memory/. MEMORY.md serves as the index. Managed via /memory. Claude automatically references stored memories in future interactions. Memory is organized into topic-specific files under the memory directory. MEMORY.md acts as an index that Claude reads first to decide which memory files to load for a given task. Memory files use plain markdown format so they are human-readable and editable. Claude adds structured headers to each file to make the content easy to index and retrieve.

features.name: @-mentions
features.summary_ja: ファイルを@で参照
features.description_ja: プロンプト内で @path/to/file と書くと、そのファイルが自動的にコンテキストに追加される。
features.summary_en: Reference any file in a prompt using @path/to/file syntax to automatically add its contents to Claude's context; Writing @path/to/file in a prompt automatically adds the file's contents to the context.
features.description_en: Writing @path/to/file in a prompt automatically adds the file's contents to the context. The fastest way to bring a specific file into scope without manually reading it first. Supports both absolute and relative paths. @-mentions can reference any file within the allowed directories. The file content is added inline to the context, making it immediately available to Claude without a separate Read call. @-mention autocomplete is available in the prompt input when you type @ followed by a path. The autocomplete dropdown shows matching files from the allowed directories.

features.name: .claude/rules/
features.summary_ja: 条件付きルールファイルをディレクトリで管理
features.description_ja: `.claude/rules/*.md` に書いたルールは CLAUDE.md と同様に自動ロード（v2.0.64追加）。frontmatter の `paths:` フィールドで特定ファイルパターンにのみ適用される条件付きルールも作成可能。InstructionsLoaded フックと連携して読み込み検知も可能。
features.summary_en: Directory for conditional rule files at .claude/rules/ that load like CLAUDE.md with optional path-based filtering; Rules in .claude/rules/*.md are auto-loaded like CLAUDE.md (added in v2.0.64).
features.description_en: Rules in .claude/rules/*.md are auto-loaded like CLAUDE.md (added in v2.0.64). A frontmatter paths: field enables conditional rules that apply only to files matching specific path patterns. Works with the InstructionsLoaded hook to detect when rules are loaded. Rules files support the same markdown and frontmatter format as CLAUDE.md. Path-conditional rules are particularly useful for enforcing language-specific coding standards only when editing files of that type. Rules files are merged with CLAUDE.md at session start. Path-conditional rules use glob patterns in the paths frontmatter field, enabling language-specific or directory-specific rule sets.

features.name: .claude/commands/
features.summary_ja: カスタムスラッシュコマンドをMarkdownで定義
features.description_ja: `.claude/commands/` ディレクトリに Markdown ファイルを置くと、その内容がプロンプトとして挿入されるカスタムスラッシュコマンドになる（v0.2.31追加）。サブディレクトリでネームスペース管理も可能（例: `frontend/fix.md` → `/frontend:fix`）。スキルより軽量な仕組みで、プロンプトテンプレートの共有に最適。
features.summary_en: Directory for custom slash commands defined as Markdown files with namespace support via subdirectory organization; Markdown files in .claude/commands/ become custom slash commands whose contents are inserted as a prompt.
features.description_en: Markdown files in .claude/commands/ become custom slash commands whose contents are inserted as a prompt (added in v0.2.31). Subdirectories enable namespace management (e.g., frontend/fix.md becomes /frontend:fix). Lighter-weight than skills and ideal for sharing prompt templates. Custom commands appear in the /skills picker alongside installed skills. They are simpler to create than full skills since they require only a markdown file with no frontmatter or tooling. Custom commands are available immediately after saving without restarting. They appear in the autocomplete list when you type / in the prompt, just like built-in commands.

features.name: --continue (-c)
features.summary_ja: 前回のセッションを継続
features.description_ja: claude --continue で直前のセッションをそのまま再開。コンテキストを失わずに続けられる。
features.summary_en: Resume the most recent session without losing context using the --continue or -c flag at startup; claude --continue resumes the most recent session as-is, without losing context.
features.description_en: claude --continue resumes the most recent session as-is, without losing context. The shortest path back to where you left off. Use --resume (-r) when you want to pick from a list of older sessions. If no previous session exists, --continue starts a new session normally. Use --resume (-r) instead when you want to select a specific session rather than always picking the most recent one. The -c shorthand is useful in shell aliases for starting Claude Code and immediately resuming your last working context. Combine with --add-dir to also restore extra directories.

features.name: --resume (-r)
features.summary_ja: 特定セッションを再開
features.description_ja: claude --resume <session-id> で過去の任意セッションを再開。
features.summary_en: Resume any past session by session ID or via an interactive picker using the --resume or -r flag; claude --resume <session-id> resumes any past session.
features.description_en: claude --resume <session-id> resumes any past session. Running without a session ID opens an interactive picker. Use --continue to resume the most recent session without needing to select. Sessions resumed with --resume start exactly where they left off, including all tool permissions and context that was active at the time the session ended or was interrupted. When using --resume without a session ID, an interactive picker shows session names, dates, and recent messages to help identify the right session. Session IDs are UUIDs shown in the resume picker and in /status.

features.name: --print (-p)
features.summary_ja: ヘッドレスモードで一発実行
features.description_ja: 対話UIを起動せずプロンプトを実行して結果を出力。スクリプト連携に。
features.summary_en: Run a single prompt non-interactively and print the result using the --print or -p flag for scripts and CI; Runs a prompt without launching the interactive UI and prints the.
features.description_en: Runs a prompt without launching the interactive UI and prints the result. Designed for script integration and CI pipelines. Combine with --dangerously-skip-permissions for fully automated runs in trusted environments. Output is written to stdout in plain text by default. Add --output-format json to get structured output for programmatic processing in CI pipelines or build scripts. The -p shorthand is commonly used in shell pipelines. Pipe git diff output into a -p prompt to have Claude review changes or write commit messages from the CLI.

features.name: --model
features.summary_ja: 使用モデルを起動時に指定
features.description_ja: claude --model claude-opus-4-7 のように、起動時に使用モデルを固定。
features.summary_en: Specify the model to use at startup with --model; overrides the default configured in settings.json; Specifies which model to use at launch, e.g., claude --model claude-opus-4-7.
features.description_en: Specifies which model to use at launch, e.g., claude --model claude-opus-4-7. Overrides the default model configured in settings.json. Use /model inside a session to switch models without restarting. Model names use the full slug format, such as claude-sonnet-4-6 or claude-opus-4-7. Use /model inside a session to switch models interactively without specifying the full slug. Using --model ensures consistent model usage in CI scripts regardless of what is configured in settings.json. Always specify the full model slug, not a short alias.

features.name: --add-dir
features.summary_ja: 追加の作業ディレクトリを指定
features.description_ja: Claude のアクセスを通常のcwd以外にも広げる。複数リポジトリを横断する作業に。
features.summary_en: Expand Claude's file access to additional directories beyond the current working directory at startup; Expands Claude's access beyond the normal cwd to additional directories.
features.description_en: Expands Claude's access beyond the normal cwd to additional directories. Useful for cross-repository tasks or monorepos where relevant code lives outside the current directory. Use /add-dir inside a session to add directories without restarting. Multiple directories can be added at launch by repeating --add-dir. This is equivalent to running /add-dir for each directory inside the session but applies before any prompts run. Directories added at launch via --add-dir are listed in /status. They are session-scoped and do not persist unless you use /add-dir --remember inside the session.

features.name: --worktree (-w)
features.summary_ja: 独立した git worktree でセッションを開始
features.description_ja: claude --worktree (-w) で隔離された git worktree 内でセッションを起動する（v2.1.49追加）。`worktree.baseRef` 設定（fresh|head）でブランチ元を origin/<default> かローカル HEAD かを選択可能。worktree 内では skills・hooks・settings.json が worktree ディレクトリからロードされる。
features.summary_en: Start a session inside an isolated git worktree using --worktree or -w, added in v2.1.49; Launches a session inside an isolated git worktree (added in v2.1.49).
features.description_en: Launches a session inside an isolated git worktree (added in v2.1.49). The worktree.baseRef setting (fresh|head) controls whether the branch base is origin/<default> or local HEAD. Skills, hooks, and settings.json are loaded from the worktree directory. The worktree is automatically cleaned up when the session ends unless you explicitly keep it. The worktree.baseRef setting controls whether Claude branches from the default remote or from local HEAD. Worktrees are cleaned up automatically when the session ends by default. Set worktree.keepOnExit in settings.json to retain worktrees for inspection after the session closes.

features.name: --dangerously-skip-permissions
features.summary_ja: 全ての許可プロンプトをバイパス（CI向け）
features.description_ja: ほぼ全操作の許可プロンプトをバイパスする。v2.1.126以降、`.claude/`・`.git/`・`.vscode/`・シェル設定ファイルへの書き込みも対象（rm -rf等の危険コマンドは引き続きプロンプト表示）。CI環境・エージェント実行の完全自動化に使う。`claude agents` にも渡せる（v2.1.142）。
features.summary_en: Bypass almost all permission prompts for CI and fully automated agent runs; still prompts for dangerous commands like rm -rf.
features.description_en: Bypasses almost all permission prompts. Since v2.1.126, this includes writes to .claude/, .git/, .vscode/, and shell config files (dangerous commands like rm -rf still prompt). Use in CI environments and fully automated agent runs. Also passable to claude agents (v2.1.142). This flag should only be used in sandboxed CI environments or trusted automation scripts. Even with this flag, a small set of destructive commands still trigger confirmation prompts. Use this flag with caution: it grants Claude broad write access to your project, including configuration files and shell scripts. Suitable only for isolated CI containers.

features.name: --plugin-dir
features.summary_ja: ローカルプラグインディレクトリを指定
features.description_ja: ローカルのプラグインディレクトリまたは .zip アーカイブを指定してプラグインを読み込む（v2.1.74）。同名のマーケットプレイス製プラグインより優先される。複数指定は `--plugin-dir` を繰り返す。`/bg` バックグラウンドセッションにも引き継がれる（v2.1.143）。
features.summary_en: Load a local plugin from a directory or .zip archive at startup, taking precedence over Marketplace plugins of the same name.
features.description_en: Specifies a local plugin directory or .zip archive to load (v2.1.74). Takes precedence over Marketplace plugins with the same name. Repeat --plugin-dir to load multiple plugins. Carried over to /bg background sessions (v2.1.143). Local plugins loaded via --plugin-dir override Marketplace plugins of the same name, making this flag ideal for developing and testing plugin updates before publishing. Use --plugin-dir during development to test plugins before publishing them to the Marketplace. Switch to the Marketplace-installed version after publishing to test the published artifact.

features.name: --mcp-config
features.summary_ja: 外部ファイルでMCPサーバー設定を指定
features.description_ja: JSON ファイルで MCP サーバー設定を指定し、プロジェクトの `.mcp.json` を上書きする。`-p` モードでは接続待ちを最大5秒に制限。`/bg` バックグラウンドセッションにも引き継がれる（v2.1.143）。`allowedMcpServers` 等の managed policy も適用される（v2.1.83修正）。
features.summary_en: Override the project .mcp.json with an external JSON file to specify MCP server configuration at startup; Specifies MCP server configuration via a JSON file, overriding the project's .mcp.json.
features.description_en: Specifies MCP server configuration via a JSON file, overriding the project's .mcp.json. In -p mode, connection wait time is capped at 5 seconds. Carried over to /bg background sessions (v2.1.143). Managed policies like allowedMcpServers are applied (fixed in v2.1.83). The JSON file format matches the .mcp.json schema. This flag is commonly used in CI to inject environment-specific MCP server configurations without modifying the committed .mcp.json. The --mcp-config flag is particularly useful in multi-environment setups where dev, staging, and production each require different MCP server endpoints in the same project.

features.name: Subagent System
features.summary_ja: サブエージェントの仕組み
features.description_ja: メインのClaudeとは別の文脈で動く専門エージェント。Task tool 経由で起動。複雑タスクの並列処理や文脈分離に。
features.summary_en: Specialized agents launched via the Task tool that run in separate contexts for parallel task processing and context isolation; Specialized agents that operate in separate contexts from the main Claude.
features.description_en: Specialized agents that operate in separate contexts from the main Claude session. Launched via the Task tool. Used for parallel processing of complex tasks and context isolation. Built-in types include Explore, Plan, and general-purpose. Subagents are isolated: they do not share memory or file state with the parent session by default. Results must be explicitly returned by the subagent and read by the parent. Subagent results are returned as plain text to the parent session. Structure the subagent's prompt to produce parseable output if you need to process results programmatically.

features.name: Explore Agent
features.summary_ja: 高速読み取り検索エージェント
features.description_ja: コードを探すための読み取り専用検索エージェント。パターン・キーワード・シンボル検索に最適。並列実行で本体の文脈を消費しない。
features.summary_en: Fast read-only search agent optimized for pattern, keyword, and symbol discovery without consuming the main context window; A read-only search agent optimized for finding code.
features.description_en: A read-only search agent optimized for finding code. Best for pattern, keyword, and symbol searches. Runs in parallel without consuming the main session's context window. Use for large codebase exploration tasks. Explore agents are optimized for speed and low context consumption. They use Glob and Grep internally and return structured results rather than prose summaries. Explore agents cache file system snapshots for fast repeated queries within the same invocation. Restart the agent if the file system changes significantly after the initial snapshot.

features.name: Plan Agent
features.summary_ja: 実装計画を設計するエージェント
features.description_ja: タスクの実装戦略を設計。ステップバイステップ計画、重要ファイル特定、アーキテクチャトレードオフ考慮。

examples.title: Plan a new feature
examples.code: Task: subagent_type=Plan で認証機能の実装計画

features.summary_en: Planning agent that designs step-by-step implementation strategies, identifies key files, and considers architectural trade-offs; Designs implementation strategies for tasks; Produces step-by-step plans, identifies key files, and considers architectural trade-offs.
features.description_en: Designs implementation strategies for tasks. Produces step-by-step plans, identifies key files, and considers architectural trade-offs. Use before implementation to reduce wasted effort on misaligned approaches. Plan agents produce detailed step-by-step plans with file references and risk assessments. The output can be fed directly into the main session as the starting context for implementation. Plan agents do not modify files, so they are safe to run in any permission mode. Use them as a read-only planning step before switching to a code-writing agent for implementation.

features.name: general-purpose Agent
features.summary_ja: 汎用エージェント
features.description_ja: 特化していない汎用エージェント。複雑な調査やマルチステップタスクに使える。
features.summary_en: Non-specialized general-purpose agent suitable for complex multi-step research and investigation tasks in a separate context; A non-specialized general-purpose agent suitable for complex investigations and multi-step tasks.
features.description_en: A non-specialized general-purpose agent suitable for complex investigations and multi-step tasks. Use when no built-in specialized agent type fits the task. Runs in a separate context to preserve the main session's window. General-purpose agents have access to the full tool set including Bash, Read, Write, and Edit. Use them for tasks that require both exploration and code changes in a single subagent. General-purpose agents are most effective for tasks that combine research and implementation in a single workflow, such as investigating a bug and writing the fix in one subagent.

features.name: Custom Subagents
features.summary_ja: 独自サブエージェントの作成
features.description_ja: .claude/agents/<name>.md にエージェント定義を置くと、自分専用のサブエージェントが作れる。
features.summary_en: Create a personal subagent by placing an agent definition Markdown file in .claude/agents/ for project-specific workflows; Place an agent definition at .claude/agents/<name>.md to create a custom subagent.
features.description_en: Place an agent definition at .claude/agents/<name>.md to create a custom subagent. The Markdown file defines the agent's behavior, available tools, and instructions. Enables project-specific specialized agents tailored to your workflow. Custom agent definitions support the same frontmatter fields as SKILL.md files, including tools, description, and instructions. They appear in the /agents picker alongside built-in types. Custom agents defined in .claude/agents/ are project-specific. For shareable agents, bundle them in a plugin so teammates can install them from the Marketplace. Test custom agents thoroughly before sharing them, since they have access to the same tool set as the main session.

features.name: Worktree Isolation
features.summary_ja: git worktreeでエージェント隔離
features.description_ja: サブエージェントを別のgit worktreeで動かしてmainと隔離。並列開発の事故防止に。
features.summary_en: Run subagents in separate git worktrees to prevent interference with the main branch during parallel development; Runs subagents in separate git worktrees to isolate them from the main branch.
features.description_en: Runs subagents in separate git worktrees to isolate them from the main branch. Prevents accidental interference during parallel development. Combines with the Task tool's isolation setting for safe concurrent experimentation. Worktree isolation creates a fresh branch for each agent, preventing merge conflicts between parallel agents working on the same codebase. Results are merged back manually after review. Worktree isolation is the safest mode for multi-agent workflows that modify overlapping files. Each agent works in its own branch, preventing overwrite conflicts.

features.name: Skills System
features.summary_ja: 再利用可能なスキルシステム
features.description_ja: .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md でスキル定義。description のキーワードでトリガーされ、Claudeが自動的に呼び出す。
features.summary_en: Reusable skill system defined in SKILL.md files that Claude automatically invokes when description keywords match user input; Defines reusable skills in .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md.
features.description_en: Defines reusable skills in .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md. Skills are automatically invoked by Claude when description keywords match the user's input. They can also be listed and selected manually via /skills. Skills are loaded at session start from .claude/skills/ and from installed plugins. The /skills command shows all available skills and lets you invoke one by name. Skills are composable: one skill can invoke another via SlashCommand. This makes it possible to build complex, multi-step automation by chaining simpler skill definitions. Delete a skill by removing its directory; the next session reload will no longer list it in /skills.

features.name: SKILL.md Structure
features.summary_ja: スキル定義ファイルの構造
features.description_ja: frontmatter に description（トリガー文）、name、tools などを書く。本文は手順・ルール・テンプレート。
features.summary_en: SKILL.md frontmatter structure with description, name, and tools fields; the description field determines automatic invocation; The frontmatter in SKILL.md contains description (trigger phrase), name, and tools fields.
features.description_en: The frontmatter in SKILL.md contains description (trigger phrase), name, and tools fields. The body contains procedures, rules, and templates. The description field is the most important: it determines when Claude automatically invokes the skill. The tools field restricts which tools the skill can use, improving safety for shared skills. The body section is freeform markdown and can include templates, examples, and step-by-step instructions. The description field uses natural language that Claude's embedding model matches against user input. Write descriptions that capture the user's intent rather than listing exact command names.

features.name: Plugin Marketplace
features.summary_ja: 公式プラグインマーケット
features.description_ja: Anthropic公式や有志によるプラグイン（スキル/エージェントのバンドル）を配布・取得できるマーケット。
features.summary_en: Official plugin marketplace for distributing and discovering plugins from Anthropic and the community; A marketplace for distributing and discovering plugins (skill and agent bundles) from Anthropic and the community.
features.description_en: A marketplace for distributing and discovering plugins (skill and agent bundles) from Anthropic and the community. Install plugins with /plugin and browse available offerings at /marketplace. Marketplace plugins are installed into ~/.claude/plugins/ and auto-updated unless pinned. Use /plugin to manage installed plugins and /reload-plugins to apply updates immediately. Marketplace plugins are sandboxed and reviewed before publication. Check the publisher name and version history before installing plugins from unknown sources. Pinned plugins do not auto-update; run /plugin update explicitly to apply a new version.

features.name: Skill Triggers
features.summary_ja: スキルの自動起動トリガー
features.description_ja: SKILL.md の description に書いたキーワードが入力にマッチすると Claude が自動でスキルを呼び出す仕組み。

examples.title: Description example
examples.code: description: PRレビューしたい時に使う

features.summary_en: Mechanism by which Claude automatically invokes a skill when keywords in the SKILL.md description field match user input; When keywords in the SKILL.md description field match user input, Claude automatically.
features.description_en: When keywords in the SKILL.md description field match user input, Claude automatically invokes the skill. This makes skills hands-free for common tasks. The description field doubles as a documentation string and a trigger specification. Trigger matching is semantic rather than exact: Claude evaluates whether the user's intent matches the description. This means triggers work even if the exact keywords are not used. Skill triggers fire based on semantic intent matching, not exact keyword matching. This makes triggers robust to rephrasing while still being specific enough to avoid false positives.

features.name: MCP Overview
features.summary_ja: Model Context Protocol
features.description_ja: 外部ツール・APIをClaudeに統合するためのオープンプロトコル。GitHub、Slack、DBなどへの橋渡し。
features.summary_en: Open protocol for integrating external tools and APIs such as GitHub, Slack, and databases into Claude via .mcp.json; An open protocol for integrating external tools and APIs into Claude.
features.description_en: An open protocol for integrating external tools and APIs into Claude. Bridges Claude to services such as GitHub, Slack, and databases. Configure servers in .mcp.json and check status with /mcp. MCP servers are defined in .mcp.json at the project root or in ~/.claude/settings.json for global access. The /mcp command shows all connected servers and their available tools. MCP tools appear alongside built-in Claude Code tools in permission prompts. Grant access to specific MCP servers in the allowedMcpServers field of settings.json for auto-approval.

features.name: MCP stdio Server
features.summary_ja: 標準入出力ベースのMCPサーバー
features.description_ja: プロセス起動して stdin/stdout でやりとりするMCPサーバー。最も一般的。Pythonやnpx で簡単に動かせる。
features.summary_en: MCP server type that communicates via stdin/stdout by spawning a process; the most common type, runnable with Python or npx.
features.description_en: An MCP server that communicates via stdin/stdout by spawning a process. The most common MCP server type. Easy to run with Python or npx. Ideal for local tools and development integrations. The stdio transport spawns the server process as a child of Claude Code, so the server inherits the environment and working directory. Use this for local CLI-based tool integrations. Stdio servers start and stop with the Claude Code session. If a stdio server crashes, Claude Code detects the failure and marks the server as disconnected in the /mcp status view.

features.name: MCP SSE Server
features.summary_ja: Server-Sent Events型のMCPサーバー
features.description_ja: HTTP経由でストリーミング通信するMCPサーバー。クラウドホスト型サービスに向く。
features.summary_en: MCP server type using Server-Sent Events over HTTP for persistent streaming communication with cloud-hosted services; An MCP server that communicates via streaming over HTTP using Server-Sent Events.
features.description_en: An MCP server that communicates via streaming over HTTP using Server-Sent Events. Suited for cloud-hosted services that need to push updates to Claude. The SSE transport enables persistent connections for long-running integrations. SSE transport maintains a persistent HTTP connection so the server can push events to Claude in real time. This transport is deprecated in favor of the HTTP transport in newer MCP versions. SSE connections are persistent, so the server stays running between Claude Code sessions if hosted externally. Reconnection on session start is automatic using the URL in .mcp.json.

features.name: MCP HTTP Server
features.summary_ja: REST/HTTP型のMCPサーバー
features.description_ja: 標準的なHTTPでやり取りするMCPサーバー。シンプルなリクエスト/レスポンスモデル。
features.summary_en: MCP server type using standard HTTP request/response for straightforward REST-based integrations without persistent connections; An MCP server that communicates over standard HTTP using a simple request/response model.
features.description_en: An MCP server that communicates over standard HTTP using a simple request/response model. Best for existing REST APIs that need to be exposed as MCP tools without the complexity of a persistent connection. The HTTP transport is the recommended modern transport for new MCP integrations. It uses standard REST semantics and works behind proxies and load balancers without special configuration. HTTP MCP servers can be deployed behind authentication proxies. Pass auth headers in the .mcp.json headers field to ensure Claude Code can authenticate with secured endpoints.

features.name: Status Line
features.summary_ja: プロンプト下部のカスタム表示
features.description_ja: git ブランチ、モデル名、コンテキスト残量など、自分の好みに合わせて統合バーを表示できる。
features.summary_en: Customizable status bar below the prompt that can display git branch, model name, context usage, and other information; Displays a customizable integration bar below the prompt showing git branch, model.
features.description_en: Displays a customizable integration bar below the prompt showing git branch, model name, remaining context, and other information of your choice. Configure it via the /statusline-setup skill. The status line is configured via the /statusline-setup skill and written to settings.json. Custom segments can display environment variables, git state, or any shell command output. The status line runs shell commands to generate dynamic segments, so it can display anything your shell can produce. Keep segments fast to avoid adding latency to the prompt.

features.name: Keybindings
features.summary_ja: キーボードショートカット
features.description_ja: ~/.claude/keybindings.json でキー割当をカスタマイズ。chord（複数キー）対応。
features.summary_en: Customize keyboard shortcuts including chord multi-key sequences by editing ~/.claude/keybindings.json; Customizes key assignments in ~/.claude/keybindings.json; Supports chord (multi-key) sequences; Use the /keybindings command or the keybindings-help skill for setup guidance.
features.description_en: Customizes key assignments in ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Supports chord (multi-key) sequences. Use the /keybindings command or the keybindings-help skill for setup guidance. Chord bindings allow sequences of two or more keys to trigger commands, enabling a large number of shortcuts without conflicting with terminal or OS keybindings. Context-aware bindings use a context field in keybindings.json to scope a binding to specific input contexts such as the prompt area or the file picker. Run /keybindings to get step-by-step guidance for editing the file correctly.

features.name: VS Code Extension
features.summary_ja: VS Code拡張機能
features.description_ja: VS CodeにClaude Codeを統合。エディタ内でClaudeと対話、現在のファイル/選択範囲を即文脈化。
features.summary_en: VS Code extension that integrates Claude Code into the editor for in-context assistance with current files and selections; Integrates Claude Code into VS Code.
features.description_en: Integrates Claude Code into VS Code. Interact with Claude inside the editor and instantly add the current file or selection to context. Install from the VS Code Marketplace or via /ide. The VS Code extension adds a Claude Code panel to the sidebar and supports inline diff viewing of AI-generated edits. File and selection context is passed automatically on request. The extension supports multiple Claude Code sessions in separate VS Code windows simultaneously. Each window has an independent session with its own history and context.

features.name: JetBrains Plugin
features.summary_ja: JetBrains IDE プラグイン
features.description_ja: IntelliJ / WebStorm / PyCharm などへClaude Codeを統合。VS Code版と同等。
features.summary_en: JetBrains plugin that integrates Claude Code into IntelliJ, WebStorm, PyCharm, and other JetBrains IDEs; Integrates Claude Code into IntelliJ, WebStorm, PyCharm, and other JetBrains IDEs.
features.description_en: Integrates Claude Code into IntelliJ, WebStorm, PyCharm, and other JetBrains IDEs. Feature parity with the VS Code extension. Install from the JetBrains Marketplace or via /ide. The JetBrains plugin mirrors the VS Code extension feature set and is updated on the same release cycle. It supports all IDEs in the JetBrains family that run on the IntelliJ platform. The JetBrains plugin updates automatically through the IDE's built-in plugin manager. Manual reinstallation is only needed if the auto-update mechanism encounters an error.

features.name: Fast Mode
features.summary_ja: 高速応答モード
features.description_ja: /fast コマンドで Opus 4.6 ベースの高速応答に切替。Opus 4.6 でのみ利用可能。
features.summary_en: High-speed response mode based on Claude Opus 4.6 toggled with the /fast command; only available on Opus 4.6; Switches to a fast response mode based on Claude Opus 4.6 using.
features.description_en: Switches to a fast response mode based on Claude Opus 4.6 using the /fast command. Only available with Opus 4.6. Trades some depth for speed. Toggle on and off as needed during a session. Fast Mode is a separate toggle from model selection: you can use Opus with or without Fast Mode. The mode trades some reasoning depth for a significant reduction in response latency. Fast Mode is ideal for iterative back-and-forth during active coding sessions. Switch back to standard mode for complex refactoring or architecture questions that benefit from deeper reasoning.
